The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite 海江田氏?（武家） など }} '''Kusakabe Clan''' (日下部氏, Kusakabe-uji) is a [[Japanese clan]] with its origins in ancient Japan. Their family crests include {{ill|Wood Sorrel Crest|ja|木瓜紋}}, {{ill|Ivy Crest|ja|蔦}}, and {{ill|Three Ginkgo Crest|ja|三つ銀杏}}. == Origins == ; Region "Kusakabe" is one of the clans associated with the [[Bemin|Be-system]] and noble families holding the {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}} title. Their names appear in various forms, such as 日部, ＃部 (where ＃ combines "日" above "下"), 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, and 久坂部. Their distribution spans regions such as {{ill|Hyūga Province|ja|日向国}}, {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国}}, {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹国}}, {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}, {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}, and others. The {{ill|Kusakabe Clan|ja|日下部氏}} of Dazaifu, Kusakabe-no-Muraji in Bungo Province, Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Chikugo Province, Kusakabe Clan of Kawachi Province, and Kusakabe Clan of Iyo Province share a common ancestor with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ; Lineage The Kusakabe Clan is thought to originate from several genealogical lines: # {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}}'s grandson, {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命}}, a member of the {{ill|Mononobe Clan|ja|物部氏}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|ja|火闌降命}}, regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hayato Clan|ja|隼人}} in {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|薩摩国}}, sharing origins with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the {{ill|Star River Rebellion|ja|星川皇子の乱}} under {{ill|Yamato Sumeragi|ja|雄略天皇}}, granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Progeny of {{ill|Prince Ōkusaka|ja|大草香皇子}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s grandson, Omoteomi no Miko (表米親王), who led the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, son of Himusubi no Mikoto, who initiated the Kusakabe-no-Muraji lineage. # Descendants of {{ill|Watake no Mikoto|ja|若多祁命}}, whose son Tasanouji founded the Kusakabe-no-Muraji of {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国造}}. # Progeny of {{ill|Amaterasu no Mikoto|ja|天照大神}}, represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite Their names appear in various forms, such as 日部, ＃部 (where ＃ combines "日" above "下"), 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, and 久坂部. The {{ill|Kusakabe Clan|ja|日下部氏}} of Dazaifu, Kusakabe-no-Muraji in Bungo Province, Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Chikugo Province, Kusakabe Clan of Kawachi Province, and Kusakabe Clan of Iyo Province share a common ancestor with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ; Lineage The Kusakabe Clan is thought to originate from several genealogical lines: # {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}}'s grandson, {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命}}, a member of the {{ill|Mononobe Clan|ja|物部氏}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|ja|火闌降命}}, regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hayato Clan|ja|隼人}} in {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|薩摩国}}, sharing origins with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the {{ill|Star River Rebellion|ja|星川皇子の乱}} under {{ill|Yamato Sumeragi|ja|雄略天皇}}, granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Progeny of {{ill|Prince Ōkusaka|ja|大草香皇子}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s grandson, Omoteomi no Miko (表米親王), who led the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, son of Himusubi no Mikoto, who initiated the Kusakabe-no-Muraji lineage. # Descendants of {{ill|Watake no Mikoto|ja|若多祁命}}, whose son Tasanouji founded the Kusakabe-no-Muraji of {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国造}}. # Progeny of {{ill|Amaterasu no Mikoto|ja|天照大神}}, represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|author-link=Ota Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans, Volume 2|publisher=Surnames Publishing|year=1934|url={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|id={{NDLBibID|47004572}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} [[Category:Kusakabe clan]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kinai region {{ill|Asakura Clan|ja|朝倉氏}}（武家）

日下氏?（武家）
海江田氏?（武家） など }} '''Kusakabe Clan''' (日下部氏, Kusakabe-uji) is a [[Japanese clan]] with its origins in ancient Japan. Their family crests include {{ill|Wood Sorrel Crest|ja|木瓜紋}}, {{ill|Ivy Crest|ja|蔦}}, and {{ill|Three Ginkgo Crest|ja|三つ銀杏}}. == Origins == ; Region "Kusakabe" is one of the clans associated with the [[Bemin|Be-system]] and noble families holding the {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}} title. Their names appear in various forms, such as 日部, ＃部 (where ＃ combines "日" above "下"), 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, and 久坂部. Their distribution spans regions such as {{ill|Hyūga Province|ja|日向国}}, {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国}}, {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹国}}, {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}, {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}, and others. The {{ill|Kusakabe Clan|ja|日下部氏}} of Dazaifu, Kusakabe-no-Muraji in Bungo Province, Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Chikugo Province, Kusakabe Clan of Kawachi Province, and Kusakabe Clan of Iyo Province share a common ancestor with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ; Lineage The Kusakabe Clan is thought to originate from several genealogical lines: # {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}}'s grandson, {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命}}, a member of the {{ill|Mononobe Clan|ja|物部氏}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|ja|火闌降命}}, regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hayato Clan|ja|隼人}} in {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|薩摩国}}, sharing origins with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the {{ill|Star River Rebellion|ja|星川皇子の乱}} under {{ill|Yamato Sumeragi|ja|雄略天皇}}, granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Progeny of {{ill|Prince Ōkusaka|ja|大草香皇子}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s grandson, Omoteomi no Miko (表米親王), who led the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, son of Himusubi no Mikoto, who initiated the Kusakabe-no-Muraji lineage. # Descendants of {{ill|Watake no Mikoto|ja|若多祁命}}, whose son Tasanouji founded the Kusakabe-no-Muraji of {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国造}}. # Progeny of {{ill|Amaterasu no Mikoto|ja|天照大神}}, represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命}}
表米親王? |種別 = {{ill|Imperial Descent|ja|皇別}}、{{ill|Kami Descent|ja|神別}} |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Otagaki Clan|ja|太田垣氏}}（{{ill|Samurai|ja|武家}}）
{{ill|Yagi Clan|ja|八木氏}}（武家）
{{ill|Asakura Clan|ja|朝倉氏}}（武家）
{{ill|Hashimoto Clan|ja|橋本氏}}（武家）
{{ill|Iwakiri Clan|ja|岩切氏}}（武家）
{{ill|Kisaichi Clan|ja|私市氏}}（武家）
日下氏?（武家）
海江田氏?（武家） など }} '''Kusakabe Clan''' (日下部氏, Kusakabe-uji) is a [[Japanese clan]] with its origins in ancient Japan. Their family crests include {{ill|Wood Sorrel Crest|ja|木瓜紋}}, {{ill|Ivy Crest|ja|蔦}}, and {{ill|Three Ginkgo Crest|ja|三つ銀杏}}. == Origins == ; Region "Kusakabe" is one of the clans associated with the [[Bemin|Be-system]] and noble families holding the {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}} title. Their names appear in various forms, such as 日部, ＃部 (where ＃ combines "日" above "下"), 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, and 久坂部. Their distribution spans regions such as {{ill|Hyūga Province|ja|日向国}}, {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国}}, {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹国}}, {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}, {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}, and others. The {{ill|Kusakabe Clan|ja|日下部氏}} of Dazaifu, Kusakabe-no-Muraji in Bungo Province, Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Chikugo Province, Kusakabe Clan of Kawachi Province, and Kusakabe Clan of Iyo Province share a common ancestor with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ; Lineage The Kusakabe Clan is thought to originate from several genealogical lines: # {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}}'s grandson, {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命}}, a member of the {{ill|Mononobe Clan|ja|物部氏}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|ja|火闌降命}}, regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hayato Clan|ja|隼人}} in {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|薩摩国}}, sharing origins with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the {{ill|Star River Rebellion|ja|星川皇子の乱}} under {{ill|Yamato Sumeragi|ja|雄略天皇}}, granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Progeny of {{ill|Prince Ōkusaka|ja|大草香皇子}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s grandson, Omoteomi no Miko (表米親王), who led the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, son of Himusubi no Mikoto, who initiated the Kusakabe-no-Muraji lineage. # Descendants of {{ill|Watake no Mikoto|ja|若多祁命}}, whose son Tasanouji founded the Kusakabe-no-Muraji of {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国造}}. # Progeny of {{ill|Amaterasu no Mikoto|ja|天照大神}}, represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|author-link=Ota Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans, Volume 2|publisher=Surnames Publishing|year=1934|url={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|id={{NDLBibID|47004572}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} [[Category:Kusakabe clan]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kinai region The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite 表米親王? |種別 = {{ill|Imperial Descent|ja|皇別}}、{{ill|Kami Descent|ja|神別}} |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Otagaki Clan|ja|太田垣氏}}（{{ill|Samurai|ja|武家}}）
{{ill|Yagi Clan|ja|八木氏}}（武家）
{{ill|Asakura Clan|ja|朝倉氏}}（武家）
{{ill|Hashimoto Clan|ja|橋本氏}}（武家）

日下氏?（武家）
海江田氏?（武家） など }} '''Kusakabe Clan''' (日下部氏, Kusakabe-uji) is a [[Japanese clan]] with its origins in ancient Japan. Their family crests include {{ill|Wood Sorrel Crest|ja|木瓜紋}}, {{ill|Ivy Crest|ja|蔦}}, and {{ill|Three Ginkgo Crest|ja|三つ銀杏}}. == Origins == ; Region "Kusakabe" is one of the clans associated with the [[Bemin|Be-system]] and noble families holding the {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}} title. Their names appear in various forms, such as 日部, ＃部 (where ＃ combines "日" above "下"), 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, and 久坂部. Their distribution spans regions such as {{ill|Hyūga Province|ja|日向国}}, {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国}}, {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹国}}, {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}, {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}, and others. The {{ill|Kusakabe Clan|ja|日下部氏}} of Dazaifu, Kusakabe-no-Muraji in Bungo Province, Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Chikugo Province, Kusakabe Clan of Kawachi Province, and Kusakabe Clan of Iyo Province share a common ancestor with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ; Lineage The Kusakabe Clan is thought to originate from several genealogical lines: # {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}}'s grandson, {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命}}, a member of the {{ill|Mononobe Clan|ja|物部氏}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|ja|火闌降命}}, regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hayato Clan|ja|隼人}} in {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|薩摩国}}, sharing origins with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the {{ill|Star River Rebellion|ja|星川皇子の乱}} under {{ill|Yamato Sumeragi|ja|雄略天皇}}, granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Progeny of {{ill|Prince Ōkusaka|ja|大草香皇子}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s grandson, Omoteomi no Miko (表米親王), who led the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, son of Himusubi no Mikoto, who initiated the Kusakabe-no-Muraji lineage. # Descendants of {{ill|Watake no Mikoto|ja|若多祁命}}, whose son Tasanouji founded the Kusakabe-no-Muraji of {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国造}}. # Progeny of {{ill|Amaterasu no Mikoto|ja|天照大神}}, represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite (Family Crest of the Tajima Kusakabe Clan) | surname = | founder = | origin = | notable_ancestor = [[Hikozahiko|Hikozahiko]], [[Saho Hiko]], [[Hikoyuki no Mikoto]], Prince Hyōmai? | type = [[Imperial clan]], [[Divine clan]] | location = | notable_members = | descendants = [[Ōtagaki Clan]] (Samurai), [[Yagi Clan]] (Samurai), [[Asakura Clan]] (Samurai), [[Hashimoto Clan]] (Samurai), [[Iwakiri Clan]] (Samurai), [[Kisaichi Clan]] (Samurai), Kusaka Clan? (Samurai), Kaieda Clan? (Samurai), among others. |氏名 = 日下部氏 |image = [[File:Japanese Crest mitumori mokkou.svg|150px]]

（但馬日下部氏の{{ill|Family Crest|ja|家紋}}） |氏姓 = |始祖 = |出自 = |氏祖 = {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}
{{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}
{{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命}}
表米親王? |種別 = {{ill|Imperial Descent|ja|皇別}}、{{ill|Kami Descent|ja|神別}} |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Otagaki Clan|ja|太田垣氏}}（{{ill|Samurai|ja|武家}}）
{{ill|Yagi Clan|ja|八木氏}}（武家）
{{ill|Asakura Clan|ja|朝倉氏}}（武家）
{{ill|Hashimoto Clan|ja|橋本氏}}（武家）
{{ill|Iwakiri Clan|ja|岩切氏}}（武家）
{{ill|Kisaichi Clan|ja|私市氏}}（武家）
日下氏?（武家）
海江田氏?（武家） など }} '''Kusakabe Clan''' (日下部氏, Kusakabe-uji) is a [[Japanese clan]] with its origins in ancient Japan. Their family crests include {{ill|Wood Sorrel Crest|ja|木瓜紋}}, {{ill|Ivy Crest|ja|蔦}}, and {{ill|Three Ginkgo Crest|ja|三つ銀杏}}. == Origins == ; Region "Kusakabe" is one of the clans associated with the [[Bemin|Be-system]] and noble families holding the {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}} title. Their names appear in various forms, such as 日部, ＃部 (where ＃ combines "日" above "下"), 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, and 久坂部. Their distribution spans regions such as {{ill|Hyūga Province|ja|日向国}}, {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国}}, {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹国}}, {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}, {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}, and others. The {{ill|Kusakabe Clan|ja|日下部氏}} of Dazaifu, Kusakabe-no-Muraji in Bungo Province, Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Chikugo Province, Kusakabe Clan of Kawachi Province, and Kusakabe Clan of Iyo Province share a common ancestor with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ; Lineage The Kusakabe Clan is thought to originate from several genealogical lines: # {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}}'s grandson, {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命}}, a member of the {{ill|Mononobe Clan|ja|物部氏}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|ja|火闌降命}}, regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hayato Clan|ja|隼人}} in {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|薩摩国}}, sharing origins with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the {{ill|Star River Rebellion|ja|星川皇子の乱}} under {{ill|Yamato Sumeragi|ja|雄略天皇}}, granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Progeny of {{ill|Prince Ōkusaka|ja|大草香皇子}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s grandson, Omoteomi no Miko (表米親王), who led the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, son of Himusubi no Mikoto, who initiated the Kusakabe-no-Muraji lineage. # Descendants of {{ill|Watake no Mikoto|ja|若多祁命}}, whose son Tasanouji founded the Kusakabe-no-Muraji of {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国造}}. # Progeny of {{ill|Amaterasu no Mikoto|ja|天照大神}}, represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|author-link=Ota Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans, Volume 2|publisher=Surnames Publishing|year=1934|url={{NDLDC|qq30938/102}}|id={{NDLBibID|47004572}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} [[Category:Kusakabe clan]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kinai region The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|author-link=Ota Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans, Volume 2|publisher=Surnames Publishing|year=1934|url={{NDLDC|qq30938/102}}|id={{NDLBibID|47004572}}}} * {{Cite （但馬日下部氏の{{ill|Family Crest|ja|家紋}}） |氏姓 = |始祖 = |出自 = |氏祖 = {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}
{{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}

表米親王? |種別 = {{ill|Imperial Descent|ja|皇別}}、{{ill|Kami Descent|ja|神別}} |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Otagaki Clan|ja|太田垣氏}}（{{ill|Samurai|ja|武家}}）
{{ill|Yagi Clan|ja|八木氏}}（武家）
{{ill|Asakura Clan|ja|朝倉氏}}（武家）
{{ill|Hashimoto Clan|ja|橋本氏}}（武家）
{{ill|Iwakiri Clan|ja|岩切氏}}（武家）
{{ill|Kisaichi Clan|ja|私市氏}}（武家）
日下氏?（武家）
海江田氏?（武家） など }} '''Kusakabe Clan''' (日下部氏, Kusakabe-uji) is a [[Japanese clan]] with its origins in ancient Japan. Their family crests include {{ill|Wood Sorrel Crest|ja|木瓜紋}}, {{ill|Ivy Crest|ja|蔦}}, and {{ill|Three Ginkgo Crest|ja|三つ銀杏}}. == Origins == ; Region "Kusakabe" is one of the clans associated with the [[Bemin|Be-system]] and noble families holding the {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}} title. Their names appear in various forms, such as 日部, ＃部 (where ＃ combines "日" above "下"), 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, and 久坂部. Their distribution spans regions such as {{ill|Hyūga Province|ja|日向国}}, {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国}}, {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹国}}, {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}, {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}, and others. The {{ill|Kusakabe Clan|ja|日下部氏}} of Dazaifu, Kusakabe-no-Muraji in Bungo Province, Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Chikugo Province, Kusakabe Clan of Kawachi Province, and Kusakabe Clan of Iyo Province share a common ancestor with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ; Lineage The Kusakabe Clan is thought to originate from several genealogical lines: # {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}}'s grandson, {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命}}, a member of the {{ill|Mononobe Clan|ja|物部氏}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|ja|火闌降命}}, regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hayato Clan|ja|隼人}} in {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|薩摩国}}, sharing origins with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the {{ill|Star River Rebellion|ja|星川皇子の乱}} under {{ill|Yamato Sumeragi|ja|雄略天皇}}, granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Progeny of {{ill|Prince Ōkusaka|ja|大草香皇子}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s grandson, Omoteomi no Miko (表米親王), who led the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, son of Himusubi no Mikoto, who initiated the Kusakabe-no-Muraji lineage. # Descendants of {{ill|Watake no Mikoto|ja|若多祁命}}, whose son Tasanouji founded the Kusakabe-no-Muraji of {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国造}}. # Progeny of {{ill|Amaterasu no Mikoto|ja|天照大神}}, represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite Mitsumori Mokkou
[[Mokkou mon|Three-Tiered Mokkou]]
(Family Crest of the Tajima Kusakabe Clan) | surname = | founder = | origin = | notable_ancestor = [[Hikozahiko|Hikozahiko]], [[Saho Hiko]], [[Hikoyuki no Mikoto]], Prince Hyōmai? | type = [[Imperial clan]], [[Divine clan]] | location = | notable_members = | descendants = [[Ōtagaki Clan]] (Samurai), [[Yagi Clan]] (Samurai), [[Asakura Clan]] (Samurai), [[Hashimoto Clan]] (Samurai), [[Iwakiri Clan]] (Samurai), [[Kisaichi Clan]] (Samurai), Kusaka Clan? (Samurai), Kaieda Clan? (Samurai), among others. |氏名 = 日下部氏 |image = [[File:Japanese Crest mitumori mokkou.svg|150px]]
みつもりもっこう
{{ill|Wood Sorrel Crest|ja|木瓜紋}}
（但馬日下部氏の{{ill|Family Crest|ja|家紋}}） |氏姓 = |始祖 = |出自 = |氏祖 = {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}
{{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}
{{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命}}
表米親王? |種別 = {{ill|Imperial Descent|ja|皇別}}、{{ill|Kami Descent|ja|神別}} |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Otagaki Clan|ja|太田垣氏}}（{{ill|Samurai|ja|武家}}）
{{ill|Yagi Clan|ja|八木氏}}（武家）
{{ill|Asakura Clan|ja|朝倉氏}}（武家）
{{ill|Hashimoto Clan|ja|橋本氏}}（武家）
{{ill|Iwakiri Clan|ja|岩切氏}}（武家）
{{ill|Kisaichi Clan|ja|私市氏}}（武家）
日下氏?（武家）
海江田氏?（武家） など }} '''Kusakabe Clan''' (日下部氏, Kusakabe-uji) is a [[Japanese clan]] with its origins in ancient Japan. Their family crests include {{ill|Wood Sorrel Crest|ja|木瓜紋}}, {{ill|Ivy Crest|ja|蔦}}, and {{ill|Three Ginkgo Crest|ja|三つ銀杏}}. == Origins == ; Region "Kusakabe" is one of the clans associated with the [[Bemin|Be-system]] and noble families holding the {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}} title. Their names appear in various forms, such as 日部, ＃部 (where ＃ combines "日" above "下"), 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, and 久坂部. Their distribution spans regions such as {{ill|Hyūga Province|ja|日向国}}, {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国}}, {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹国}}, {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}, {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}, and others. The {{ill|Kusakabe Clan|ja|日下部氏}} of Dazaifu, Kusakabe-no-Muraji in Bungo Province, Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Chikugo Province, Kusakabe Clan of Kawachi Province, and Kusakabe Clan of Iyo Province share a common ancestor with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ; Lineage The Kusakabe Clan is thought to originate from several genealogical lines: # {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}}'s grandson, {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命}}, a member of the {{ill|Mononobe Clan|ja|物部氏}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|ja|火闌降命}}, regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hayato Clan|ja|隼人}} in {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|薩摩国}}, sharing origins with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the {{ill|Star River Rebellion|ja|星川皇子の乱}} under {{ill|Yamato Sumeragi|ja|雄略天皇}}, granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Progeny of {{ill|Prince Ōkusaka|ja|大草香皇子}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s grandson, Omoteomi no Miko (表米親王), who led the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, son of Himusubi no Mikoto, who initiated the Kusakabe-no-Muraji lineage. # Descendants of {{ill|Watake no Mikoto|ja|若多祁命}}, whose son Tasanouji founded the Kusakabe-no-Muraji of {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国造}}. # Progeny of {{ill|Amaterasu no Mikoto|ja|天照大神}}, represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|author-link=Ota Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans, Volume 2|publisher=Surnames Publishing|year=1934|url={{NDLDC|qq30938/102}}|id={{NDLBibID|47004572}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} [[Category:Kusakabe clan]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kinai region 海江田氏?（武家） など }} '''Kusakabe Clan''' (日下部氏, Kusakabe-uji) is a [[Japanese clan]] with its origins in ancient Japan. Their family crests include {{ill|Wood Sorrel Crest|ja|木瓜紋}}, {{ill|Ivy Crest|ja|蔦}}, and {{ill|Three Ginkgo Crest|ja|三つ銀杏}}. == Origins == ; Region "Kusakabe" is one of the clans associated with the [[Bemin|Be-system]] and noble families holding the {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}} title. Their names appear in various forms, such as 日部, ＃部 (where ＃ combines "日" above "下"), 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, and 久坂部. Their distribution spans regions such as {{ill|Hyūga Province|ja|日向国}}, {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国}}, {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹国}}, {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}, {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}, and others. The {{ill|Kusakabe Clan|ja|日下部氏}} of Dazaifu, Kusakabe-no-Muraji in Bungo Province, Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Chikugo Province, Kusakabe Clan of Kawachi Province, and Kusakabe Clan of Iyo Province share a common ancestor with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ; Lineage The Kusakabe Clan is thought to originate from several genealogical lines: # {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}}'s grandson, {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命}}, a member of the {{ill|Mononobe Clan|ja|物部氏}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|ja|火闌降命}}, regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hayato Clan|ja|隼人}} in {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|薩摩国}}, sharing origins with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the {{ill|Star River Rebellion|ja|星川皇子の乱}} under {{ill|Yamato Sumeragi|ja|雄略天皇}}, granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Progeny of {{ill|Prince Ōkusaka|ja|大草香皇子}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s grandson, Omoteomi no Miko (表米親王), who led the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, son of Himusubi no Mikoto, who initiated the Kusakabe-no-Muraji lineage. # Descendants of {{ill|Watake no Mikoto|ja|若多祁命}}, whose son Tasanouji founded the Kusakabe-no-Muraji of {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国造}}. # Progeny of {{ill|Amaterasu no Mikoto|ja|天照大神}}, represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite Their distribution spans regions such as {{ill|Hyūga Province|ja|日向国}}, {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国}}, {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹国}}, {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}, {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}, and others. The {{ill|Kusakabe Clan|ja|日下部氏}} of Dazaifu, Kusakabe-no-Muraji in Bungo Province, Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Chikugo Province, Kusakabe Clan of Kawachi Province, and Kusakabe Clan of Iyo Province share a common ancestor with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ; Lineage The Kusakabe Clan is thought to originate from several genealogical lines: # {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}}'s grandson, {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命}}, a member of the {{ill|Mononobe Clan|ja|物部氏}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|ja|火闌降命}}, regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hayato Clan|ja|隼人}} in {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|薩摩国}}, sharing origins with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province }}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the {{ill|Star River Rebellion|ja|星川皇子の乱}} under {{ill|Yamato Sumeragi|ja|雄略天皇}}, granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Progeny of {{ill|Prince Ōkusaka|ja|大草香皇子}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s grandson, Omoteomi no Miko (表米親王), who led the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, son of Himusubi no Mikoto, who initiated the Kusakabe-no-Muraji lineage. # Descendants of {{ill|Watake no Mikoto|ja|若多祁命}}, whose son Tasanouji founded the Kusakabe-no-Muraji of {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国造}}. # Progeny of {{ill|Amaterasu no Mikoto|ja|天照大神}}, represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite 海江田氏?（武家） など }} '''Kusakabe Clan''' (日下部氏, Kusakabe-uji) is a [[Japanese clan]] with its origins in ancient Japan. Their family crests include {{ill|Wood Sorrel Crest|ja|木瓜紋}}, {{ill|Ivy Crest|ja|蔦}}, and {{ill|Three Ginkgo Crest|ja|三つ銀杏}}. == Origins == ; Region "Kusakabe" is one of the clans associated with the [[Bemin|Be-system]] and noble families holding the {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}} title. Their names appear in various forms, such as 日部, ＃部 (where ＃ combines "日" above "下"), 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, and 久坂部. Their distribution spans regions such as {{ill|Hyūga Province|ja|日向国}}, {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国}}, {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹国}}, {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}, {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}, and others. The {{ill|Kusakabe Clan|ja|日下部氏}} of Dazaifu, Kusakabe-no-Muraji in Bungo Province, Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Chikugo Province, Kusakabe Clan of Kawachi Province, and Kusakabe Clan of Iyo Province share a common ancestor with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ; Lineage The Kusakabe Clan is thought to originate from several genealogical lines: # {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}}'s grandson, {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命}}, a member of the {{ill|Mononobe Clan|ja|物部氏}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|ja|火闌降命}}, regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hayato Clan|ja|隼人}} in {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|Satsuma Province }}, sharing origins with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province }}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the {{ill|Star River Rebellion|ja|星川皇子の乱}} under {{ill|Yamato Sumeragi|ja|雄略天皇}}, granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Progeny of {{ill|Prince Ōkusaka|ja|大草香皇子}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s grandson, Omoteomi no Miko (表米親王), who led the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, son of Himusubi no Mikoto, who initiated the Kusakabe-no-Muraji lineage. # Descendants of {{ill|Watake no Mikoto|ja|若多祁命}}, whose son Tasanouji founded the Kusakabe-no-Muraji of {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国造}}. # Progeny of {{ill|Amaterasu no Mikoto|ja|天照大神}}, represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite {{Confusion|日下部氏 (日向国)|日下部}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | clan_name = Kusakabe Clan (日下部氏) | image = [[File:Japanese Crest mitumori mokkou.svg|150px]]
Mitsumori Mokkou
[[Mokkou mon|Three-Tiered Mokkou]]
(Family Crest of the Tajima Kusakabe Clan) | surname = | founder = | origin = | notable_ancestor = [[Hikozahiko|Hikozahiko]], [[Saho Hiko]], [[Hikoyuki no Mikoto]], Prince Hyōmai? | type = [[Imperial clan]], [[Divine clan]] | location = | notable_members = | descendants = [[Ōtagaki Clan]] (Samurai), [[Yagi Clan]] (Samurai), [[Asakura Clan]] (Samurai), [[Hashimoto Clan]] (Samurai), [[Iwakiri Clan]] (Samurai), [[Kisaichi Clan]] (Samurai), Kusaka Clan? (Samurai), Kaieda Clan? (Samurai), among others. |氏名 = 日下部氏 |image = [[File:Japanese Crest mitumori mokkou.svg|150px]]
みつもりもっこう
{{ill|Wood Sorrel Crest|ja|木瓜紋}}
（但馬日下部氏の{{ill|Family Crest|ja|家紋}}） |氏姓 = |始祖 = |出自 = |氏祖 = {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}
{{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}
{{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命}}
表米親王? |種別 = {{ill|Imperial Descent|ja|皇別}}、{{ill|Kami Descent|ja|神別}} |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Otagaki Clan|ja|太田垣氏}}（{{ill|Samurai|ja|武家}}）
{{ill|Yagi Clan|ja|八木氏}}（武家）
{{ill|Asakura Clan|ja|朝倉氏}}（武家）
{{ill|Hashimoto Clan|ja|橋本氏}}（武家）
{{ill|Iwakiri Clan|ja|岩切氏}}（武家）
{{ill|Kisaichi Clan|ja|私市氏}}（武家）
日下氏?（武家）
海江田氏?（武家） など }} '''Kusakabe Clan''' (日下部氏, Kusakabe-uji) is a [[Japanese clan]] with its origins in ancient Japan. Their family crests include {{ill|Wood Sorrel Crest|ja|木瓜紋}}, {{ill|Ivy Crest|ja|蔦}}, and {{ill|Three Ginkgo Crest|ja|三つ銀杏}}. == Origins == ; Region "Kusakabe" is one of the clans associated with the [[Bemin|Be-system]] and noble families holding the {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}} title. Their names appear in various forms, such as 日部, ＃部 (where ＃ combines "日" above "下"), 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, and 久坂部. Their distribution spans regions such as {{ill|Hyūga Province|ja|日向国}}, {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国}}, {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹国}}, {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}, {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}, and others. The {{ill|Kusakabe Clan|ja|日下部氏}} of Dazaifu, Kusakabe-no-Muraji in Bungo Province, Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Chikugo Province, Kusakabe Clan of Kawachi Province, and Kusakabe Clan of Iyo Province share a common ancestor with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ; Lineage The Kusakabe Clan is thought to originate from several genealogical lines: # {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}}'s grandson, {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命}}, a member of the {{ill|Mononobe Clan|ja|物部氏}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|ja|火闌降命}}, regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hayato Clan|ja|隼人}} in {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|Satsuma Province }}, sharing origins with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province }}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the {{ill|Star River Rebellion|ja|星川皇子の乱}} under {{ill|Yamato Sumeragi|ja|雄略天皇}}, granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Progeny of {{ill|Prince Ōkusaka|ja|大草香皇子}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s grandson, Omoteomi no Miko (表米親王), who led the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, son of Himusubi no Mikoto, who initiated the Kusakabe-no-Muraji lineage. # Descendants of {{ill|Watake no Mikoto|ja|若多祁命}}, whose son Tasanouji founded the Kusakabe-no-Muraji of {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国造}}. # Progeny of {{ill|Amaterasu no Mikoto|ja|天照大神}}, represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|author-link=Ota Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans, Volume 2|publisher=Surnames Publishing|year=1934|url={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|id={{NDLBibID|47004572}}}} * {{Cite (Family Crest of the Tajima Kusakabe Clan) | surname = | founder = | origin = | notable_ancestor = [[Hikozahiko|Hikozahiko]], [[Saho Hiko]], [[Hikoyuki no Mikoto]], Prince Hyōmai? | type = [[Imperial clan]], [[Divine clan]] | location = | notable_members = | descendants = [[Ōtagaki Clan]] (Samurai), [[Yagi Clan]] (Samurai), [[Asakura Clan]] (Samurai), [[Hashimoto Clan]] (Samurai), [[Iwakiri Clan]] (Samurai), [[Kisaichi Clan]] (Samurai), Kusaka Clan? (Samurai), Kaieda Clan? (Samurai), among others. |氏名 = 日下部氏 |image = [[File:Japanese Crest mitumori mokkou.svg|150px]]

{{ill|Wood Sorrel Crest|ja|木瓜紋}}
（但馬日下部氏の{{ill|Family Crest|ja|家紋}}） |氏姓 = |始祖 = |出自 = |氏祖 = {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}
{{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}
{{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命}}
表米親王? |種別 = {{ill|Imperial Descent|ja|皇別}}、{{ill|Kami Descent|ja|神別}} |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Otagaki Clan|ja|太田垣氏}}（{{ill|Samurai|ja|武家}}）
{{ill|Yagi Clan|ja|八木氏}}（武家）
{{ill|Asakura Clan|ja|朝倉氏}}（武家）
{{ill|Hashimoto Clan|ja|橋本氏}}（武家）
{{ill|Iwakiri Clan|ja|岩切氏}}（武家）
{{ill|Kisaichi Clan|ja|私市氏}}（武家）
日下氏?（武家）
海江田氏?（武家） など }} '''Kusakabe Clan''' (日下部氏, Kusakabe-uji) is a [[Japanese clan]] with its origins in ancient Japan. Their family crests include {{ill|Wood Sorrel Crest|ja|木瓜紋}}, {{ill|Ivy Crest|ja|蔦}}, and {{ill|Three Ginkgo Crest|ja|三つ銀杏}}. == Origins == ; Region "Kusakabe" is one of the clans associated with the [[Bemin|Be-system]] and noble families holding the {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}} title. Their names appear in various forms, such as 日部, ＃部 (where ＃ combines "日" above "下"), 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, and 久坂部. Their distribution spans regions such as {{ill|Hyūga Province|ja|日向国}}, {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国}}, {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹国}}, {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}, {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}, and others. The {{ill|Kusakabe Clan|ja|日下部氏}} of Dazaifu, Kusakabe-no-Muraji in Bungo Province, Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Chikugo Province, Kusakabe Clan of Kawachi Province, and Kusakabe Clan of Iyo Province share a common ancestor with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ; Lineage The Kusakabe Clan is thought to originate from several genealogical lines: # {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}}'s grandson, {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命}}, a member of the {{ill|Mononobe Clan|ja|物部氏}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|ja|火闌降命}}, regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hayato Clan|ja|隼人}} in {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|Satsuma Province }}, sharing origins with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province }}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the {{ill|Star River Rebellion|ja|星川皇子の乱}} under {{ill|Yamato Sumeragi|ja|雄略天皇}}, granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Progeny of {{ill|Prince Ōkusaka|ja|大草香皇子}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s grandson, Omoteomi no Miko (表米親王), who led the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, son of Himusubi no Mikoto, who initiated the Kusakabe-no-Muraji lineage. # Descendants of {{ill|Watake no Mikoto|ja|若多祁命}}, whose son Tasanouji founded the Kusakabe-no-Muraji of {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国造}}. # Progeny of {{ill|Amaterasu no Mikoto|ja|天照大神}}, represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|author-link=Ota Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans, Volume 2|publisher=Surnames Publishing|year=1934|url={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|id={{NDLBibID|47004572}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} [[Category:Kusakabe clan]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kinai region 日下氏?（武家）
海江田氏?（武家） など }} '''Kusakabe Clan''' (日下部氏, Kusakabe-uji) is a [[Japanese clan]] with its origins in ancient Japan. Their family crests include {{ill|Wood Sorrel Crest|ja|木瓜紋}}, {{ill|Ivy Crest|ja|蔦}}, and {{ill|Three Ginkgo Crest|ja|三つ銀杏}}. == Origins == ; Region "Kusakabe" is one of the clans associated with the [[Bemin|Be-system]] and noble families holding the {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}} title. Their names appear in various forms, such as 日部, ＃部 (where ＃ combines "日" above "下"), 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, and 久坂部. Their distribution spans regions such as {{ill|Hyūga Province|ja|日向国}}, {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国}}, {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹国}}, {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}, {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}, and others. The {{ill|Kusakabe Clan|ja|日下部氏}} of Dazaifu, Kusakabe-no-Muraji in Bungo Province, Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Chikugo Province, Kusakabe Clan of Kawachi Province, and Kusakabe Clan of Iyo Province share a common ancestor with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ; Lineage The Kusakabe Clan is thought to originate from several genealogical lines: # {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}}'s grandson, {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命}}, a member of the {{ill|Mononobe Clan|ja|物部氏}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|ja|火闌降命}}, regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hayato Clan|ja|隼人}} in {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|Satsuma Province }}, sharing origins with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province }}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the {{ill|Star River Rebellion|ja|星川皇子の乱}} under {{ill|Yamato Sumeragi|ja|雄略天皇}}, granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Progeny of {{ill|Prince Ōkusaka|ja|大草香皇子}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s grandson, Omoteomi no Miko (表米親王), who led the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, son of Himusubi no Mikoto, who initiated the Kusakabe-no-Muraji lineage. # Descendants of {{ill|Watake no Mikoto|ja|若多祁命}}, whose son Tasanouji founded the Kusakabe-no-Muraji of {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国造}}. # Progeny of {{ill|Amaterasu no Mikoto|ja|天照大神}}, represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite Their distribution spans regions such as {{ill|Hyūga Province|ja|日向国}}, {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国}}, {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹国}}, {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}, {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}, and others. The {{ill|Kusakabe Clan|ja|日下部氏}} of Dazaifu, Kusakabe-no-Muraji in Bungo Province, Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Chikugo Province, Kusakabe Clan of Kawachi Province, and Kusakabe Clan of Iyo Province share a common ancestor with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ; Lineage The Kusakabe Clan is thought to originate from several genealogical lines: # {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}}'s grandson, {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命}}, a member of the {{ill|Mononobe Clan|ja|物部氏}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|ja|火闌降命}}, regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hayato Clan|ja|隼人}} in {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|Satsuma Province }}, sharing origins with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province }}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the {{ill|Star River Rebellion|ja|星川皇子の乱}} under {{ill|Yamato Sumeragi|ja|雄略天皇}}, granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Progeny of {{ill|Prince Ōkusaka|ja|大草香皇子}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s grandson, Omoteomi no Miko (表米親王), who led the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, son of Himusubi no Mikoto, who initiated the Kusakabe-no-Muraji lineage. # Descendants of {{ill|Watake no Mikoto|ja|若多祁命}}, whose son Tasanouji founded the Kusakabe-no-Muraji of {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国造}}. # Progeny of {{ill|Amaterasu no Mikoto|ja|天照大神}}, represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite 海江田氏?（武家） など }} '''Kusakabe Clan''' (日下部氏, Kusakabe-uji) is a [[Japanese clan]] with its origins in ancient Japan. Their family crests include {{ill|Wood Sorrel Crest|ja|木瓜紋}}, {{ill|Ivy Crest|ja|蔦}}, and {{ill|Three Ginkgo Crest|ja|三つ銀杏}}. == Origins == ; Region "Kusakabe" is one of the clans associated with the [[Bemin|Be-system]] and noble families holding the {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}} title. Their names appear in various forms, such as 日部, ＃部 (where ＃ combines "日" above "下"), 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, and 久坂部. Their distribution spans regions such as {{ill|Hyūga Province|ja|日向国}}, {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国}}, {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹国}}, {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}, {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}, and others. The {{ill|Kusakabe Clan|ja|日下部氏}} of Dazaifu, Kusakabe-no-Muraji in Bungo Province, Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Chikugo Province, Kusakabe Clan of Kawachi Province, and Kusakabe Clan of Iyo Province share a common ancestor with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ; Lineage The Kusakabe Clan is thought to originate from several genealogical lines: # {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}}'s grandson, {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命}}, a member of the {{ill|Mononobe Clan|ja|物部氏}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|ja|火闌降命}}, regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hayato Clan|ja|隼人}} in {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|Satsuma Province }}, sharing origins with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province }}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the {{ill|Star River Rebellion|ja|星川皇子の乱}} under {{ill|Yamato Sumeragi|ja|雄略天皇}}, granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Progeny of {{ill|Prince Ōkusaka|ja|大草香皇子}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s grandson, Omoteomi no Miko (表米親王), who led the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, son of Himusubi no Mikoto, who initiated the Kusakabe-no-Muraji lineage. # Descendants of {{ill|Watake no Mikoto|ja|若多祁命}}, whose son Tasanouji founded the Kusakabe-no-Muraji of {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国造}}. # Progeny of {{ill|Amaterasu no Mikoto|ja|天照大神}}, represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|author-link=Ota {{Confusion|日下部氏 (日向国)|日下部}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | clan_name = Kusakabe Clan (日下部氏) | image = [[File:Japanese Crest mitumori mokkou.svg|150px]]
Mitsumori Mokkou
[[Mokkou mon|Three-Tiered Mokkou]]
(Family Crest of the Tajima Kusakabe Clan) | surname = | founder = | origin = | notable_ancestor = [[Hikozahiko|Hikozahiko]], [[Saho Hiko]], [[Hikoyuki no Mikoto]], Prince Hyōmai? | type = [[Imperial clan]], [[Divine clan]] | location = | notable_members = | descendants = [[Ōtagaki Clan]] (Samurai), [[Yagi Clan]] (Samurai), [[Asakura Clan]] (Samurai), [[Hashimoto Clan]] (Samurai), [[Iwakiri Clan]] (Samurai), [[Kisaichi Clan]] (Samurai), Kusaka Clan? (Samurai), Kaieda Clan? (Samurai), among others. |氏名 = 日下部氏 |image = [[File:Japanese Crest mitumori mokkou.svg|150px]]
みつもりもっこう
{{ill|Wood Sorrel Crest|ja|木瓜紋}}
（但馬日下部氏の{{ill|Family Crest|ja|家紋}}） |氏姓 = |始祖 = |出自 = |氏祖 = {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}
{{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}
{{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命}}
表米親王? |種別 = {{ill|Imperial Descent|ja|皇別}}、{{ill|Kami Descent|ja|神別}} |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Otagaki Clan|ja|太田垣氏}}（{{ill|Samurai|ja|武家}}）
{{ill|Yagi Clan|ja|八木氏}}（武家）
{{ill|Asakura Clan|ja|朝倉氏}}（武家）
{{ill|Hashimoto Clan|ja|橋本氏}}（武家）
{{ill|Iwakiri Clan|ja|岩切氏}}（武家）
{{ill|Kisaichi Clan|ja|私市氏}}（武家）
日下氏?（武家）
海江田氏?（武家） など }} '''Kusakabe Clan''' (日下部氏, Kusakabe-uji) is a [[Japanese clan]] with its origins in ancient Japan. Their family crests include {{ill|Wood Sorrel Crest|ja|木瓜紋}}, {{ill|Ivy Crest|ja|蔦}}, and {{ill|Three Ginkgo Crest|ja|三つ銀杏}}. == Origins == ; Region "Kusakabe" is one of the clans associated with the [[Bemin|Be-system]] and noble families holding the {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}} title. Their names appear in various forms, such as 日部, ＃部 (where ＃ combines "日" above "下"), 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, and 久坂部. Their distribution spans regions such as {{ill|Hyūga Province|ja|日向国}}, {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国}}, {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹国}}, {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}, {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}, and others. The {{ill|Kusakabe Clan|ja|日下部氏}} of Dazaifu, Kusakabe-no-Muraji in Bungo Province, Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Chikugo Province, Kusakabe Clan of Kawachi Province, and Kusakabe Clan of Iyo Province share a common ancestor with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ; Lineage The Kusakabe Clan is thought to originate from several genealogical lines: # {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}}'s grandson, {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命}}, a member of the {{ill|Mononobe Clan|ja|物部氏}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|ja|火闌降命}}, regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hayato Clan|ja|隼人}} in {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|Satsuma Province }}, sharing origins with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province }}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the {{ill|Star River Rebellion|ja|星川皇子の乱}} under {{ill|Yamato Sumeragi|ja|雄略天皇}}, granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Progeny of {{ill|Prince Ōkusaka|ja|大草香皇子}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s grandson, Omoteomi no Miko (表米親王), who led the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, son of Himusubi no Mikoto, who initiated the Kusakabe-no-Muraji lineage. # Descendants of {{ill|Watake no Mikoto|ja|若多祁命}}, whose son Tasanouji founded the Kusakabe-no-Muraji of {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国造}}. # Progeny of {{ill|Amaterasu no Mikoto|ja|天照大神}}, represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|author-link=Ota Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans, Volume 2|publisher=Surnames Publishing|year=1934|url={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|id={{NDLBibID|47004572}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[ja:日下部氏]] [[Category:japanese 日下氏?（武家）
海江田氏?（武家） など }} '''Kusakabe Clan''' (日下部氏, Kusakabe-uji) is a [[Japanese clan]] with its origins in ancient Japan. Their family crests include {{ill|Wood Sorrel Crest|ja|木瓜紋|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ivy Crest|ja|蔦|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Three Ginkgo Crest|ja|三つ銀杏|12=draft}}. == Origins == ; Region "Kusakabe" is one of the clans associated with the [[Bemin|Be-system]] and noble families holding the {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣|12=draft}} title. Their names appear in various forms, such as 日部, ＃部 (where ＃ combines "日" above "下"), 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, and 久坂部. Their distribution spans regions such as {{ill|Hyūga Province|ja|日向国|12=draft}}, {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹国|12=draft}}, {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province|12=draft}}, and others. The {{ill|Kusakabe Clan|ja|日下部氏|12=draft}} of Dazaifu, Kusakabe-no-Muraji in Bungo Province, Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Chikugo Province, Kusakabe Clan of Kawachi Province, and Kusakabe Clan of Iyo Province share a common ancestor with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部|12=draft}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ; Lineage The Kusakabe Clan is thought to originate from several genealogical lines: # {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=draft}}'s grandson, {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命|12=draft}}, a member of the {{ill|Mononobe Clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|ja|火闌降命|12=draft}}, regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hayato Clan|ja|隼人|12=draft}} in {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|Satsuma Province|12=draft}}, sharing origins with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部|12=draft}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=draft}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|12=draft}}, who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=draft}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the {{ill|Star River Rebellion|ja|星川皇子の乱|12=draft}} under {{ill|Yamato Sumeragi|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft}}, granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王|12=draft}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造|12=draft}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=draft}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Progeny of {{ill|Prince Ōkusaka|ja|大草香皇子|12=draft}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|12=draft}}'s grandson, Omoteomi no Miko (表米親王), who led the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|12=draft}}, son of Himusubi no Mikoto, who initiated the Kusakabe-no-Muraji lineage. # Descendants of {{ill|Watake no Mikoto|ja|若多祁命|12=draft}}, whose son Tasanouji founded the Kusakabe-no-Muraji of {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国造|12=draft}}. # Progeny of {{ill|Amaterasu no Mikoto|ja|天照大神|12=draft}}, represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫|12=draft}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|author-link=Ota Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans, Volume 2|publisher=Surnames Publishing|year=1934|url={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|id={{NDLBibID|47004572}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[ja:日下部氏]] [[sn:Kusakabe clan]] [[en:Kusakabe clan]] [[sn:usakabe clan]]
