Their names appear in various forms, such as 日部, ＃部 (where ＃ combines "日" above "下"), 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, and 久坂部. Their distribution spans regions such as {{ill|Hyūga Province|ja|日向国|12=draft}}, {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹国|12=draft}}, {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province|12=draft}}, and others. The {{ill|Kusakabe Clan|ja|日下部氏|12=draft}} of Dazaifu, Kusakabe-no-Muraji in Bungo Province, Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Chikugo Province, Kusakabe Clan of Kawachi Province, and Kusakabe Clan of Iyo Province share a common ancestor with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部|12=draft}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ; Lineage The Kusakabe Clan is thought to originate from several genealogical lines: # {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=draft}}'s grandson, {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命|12=draft}}, a member of the {{ill|Mononobe Clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|ja|火闌降命|12=draft}}, regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hayato Clan|ja|隼人|12=draft}} in {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|Satsuma Province|12=draft}}, sharing origins with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部|12=draft}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=draft}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|12=draft}}, who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=draft}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the {{ill|Star River Rebellion|ja|星川皇子の乱|1=Prince Hoshikawa Rebellion|12=draft}} under {{ill|Yamato Sumeragi|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft}}, granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王|12=draft}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造|12=draft}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=draft}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Progeny of {{ill|Prince Ōkusaka|ja|大草香皇子|12=draft}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|12=draft}}'s grandson, Omoteomi no Miko (表米親王), who led the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|12=draft}}, son of Himusubi no Mikoto, who initiated the Kusakabe-no-Muraji lineage. # Descendants of {{ill|Watake no Mikoto|ja|若多祁命|12=draft}}, whose son Tasanouji founded the Kusakabe-no-Muraji of {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国造|12=draft}}. # Progeny of {{ill|Amaterasu no Mikoto|ja|天照大神|12=draft}}, represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫|12=draft}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|author-link=Ota Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans, Volume 2|publisher=Surnames Publishing|year=1934|url={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|id={{NDLBibID|47004572}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[ja:日下部氏]] [[sn:Kusakabe clan]] [[en:Kusakabe clan]] [[sn:usakabe clan]]

Their family crests include {{ill|Wood Sorrel Crest|ja|木瓜紋|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ivy Crest|ja|蔦|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Three Ginkgo Crest|ja|三つ銀杏|12=draft}}. == Origins == ; Region "Kusakabe" is one of the clans associated with the [[Bemin|Be-system]] and noble families holding the {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣|12=draft}} title. Their names appear in various forms, such as 日部, ＃部 (where ＃ combines "日" above "下"), 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, and 久坂部. Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=draft}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|12=draft}}, who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=draft}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the {{ill|Star River Rebellion|ja|星川皇子の乱|1=Prince Hoshikawa Rebellion|12=draft}} under {{ill|Yamato Sumeragi|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft}}, granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王|12=draft}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造|12=draft}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=draft}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Their distribution spans regions such as {{ill|Hyūga Province|ja|日向国|12=draft}}, {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹国|12=draft}}, {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province|12=draft}}, and others. The {{ill|Kusakabe Clan|ja|日下部氏|12=draft}} of Dazaifu, Kusakabe-no-Muraji in Bungo Province, Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Chikugo Province, Kusakabe Clan of Kawachi Province, and Kusakabe Clan of Iyo Province share a common ancestor with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部|12=draft}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ; Lineage The Kusakabe Clan is thought to originate from several genealogical lines: # {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=draft}}'s grandson, {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命|12=draft}}, a member of the {{ill|Mononobe Clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|ja|火闌降命|12=draft}}, regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hayato Clan|ja|隼人|12=draft}} in {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|Satsuma Province|12=draft}}, sharing origins with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部|12=draft}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=draft}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|12=draft}}, who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=draft}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the {{ill|Star River Rebellion|ja|星川皇子の乱|1=Prince Hoshikawa Rebellion|12=draft}} under {{ill|Yamato Sumeragi|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft}}, granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王|12=draft}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造|12=draft}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=draft}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Progeny of {{ill|Prince Ōkusaka|ja|大草香皇子|12=draft}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|12=draft}}'s grandson, Omoteomi no Miko (表米親王), who led the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|12=draft}}, son of Himusubi no Mikoto, who initiated the Kusakabe-no-Muraji lineage. # Descendants of {{ill|Watake no Mikoto|ja|若多祁命|12=draft}}, whose son Tasanouji founded the Kusakabe-no-Muraji of {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国造|12=draft}}. # Progeny of {{ill|Amaterasu no Mikoto|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft}}, represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫|12=draft}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|author-link=Ota Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans, Volume 2|publisher=Surnames Publishing|year=1934|url={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|id={{NDLBibID|47004572}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[ja:日下部氏]] [[sn:Kusakabe clan]] [[en:Kusakabe clan]] [[sn:usakabe clan]]

(Samurai), Kaieda Clan? (Samurai), among others. |氏名 = 日下部氏 |image = [[File:Japanese Crest mitumori mokkou.svg|150px]]

表米親王? |種別 = {{ill|Imperial Descent|ja|皇別|12=draft}}、{{ill|Kami Descent|ja|神別|12=draft}} |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Otagaki Clan|ja|太田垣氏|12=draft}}（{{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|12=draft}}）
{{ill|Yagi Clan|ja|八木氏|12=draft}}（武家）
{{ill|Asakura Clan|ja|朝倉氏|12=draft}}（武家）
{{ill|Hashimoto Clan|ja|橋本氏|12=draft}}（武家）
{{ill|Iwakiri Clan|ja|岩切氏|12=draft}}（武家）
{{ill|Kisaichi Clan|ja|私市氏|12=draft}}（武家）
日下氏?（武家）
海江田氏?（武家） など }} '''Kusakabe Clan''' (日下部氏, Kusakabe-uji) is a [[Japanese clan]] with its origins in ancient Japan. Their family crests include {{ill|Wood Sorrel Crest|ja|木瓜紋|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ivy Crest|ja|蔦|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Three Ginkgo Crest|ja|三つ銀杏|12=draft}}. == Origins == ; Region "Kusakabe" is one of the clans associated with the [[Bemin|Be-system]] and noble families holding the {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣|12=draft}} title. Their names appear in various forms, such as 日部, ＃部 (where ＃ combines "日" above "下"), 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, and 久坂部. Their distribution spans regions such as {{ill|Hyūga Province|ja|日向国|12=draft}}, {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹国|12=draft}}, {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province|12=draft}}, and others. The {{ill|Kusakabe Clan|ja|日下部氏|12=draft}} of Dazaifu, Kusakabe-no-Muraji in Bungo Province, Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Chikugo Province, Kusakabe Clan of Kawachi Province, and Kusakabe Clan of Iyo Province share a common ancestor with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部|12=draft}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ; Lineage The Kusakabe Clan is thought to originate from several genealogical lines: # {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=draft}}'s grandson, {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命|12=draft}}, a member of the {{ill|Mononobe Clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|ja|火闌降命|12=draft}}, regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hayato Clan|ja|隼人|12=draft}} in {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|Satsuma Province|12=draft}}, sharing origins with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部|12=draft}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=draft}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|12=draft}}, who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=draft}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the {{ill|Star River Rebellion|ja|星川皇子の乱|1=Prince Hoshikawa Rebellion|12=draft}} under {{ill|Yamato Sumeragi|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft}}, granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王|12=draft}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造|12=draft}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=draft}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Progeny of {{ill|Prince Ōkusaka|ja|大草香皇子|12=draft}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|12=draft}}'s grandson, Omoteomi no Miko (表米親王), who led the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|12=draft}}, son of Himusubi no Mikoto, who initiated the Kusakabe-no-Muraji lineage. # Descendants of {{ill|Watake no Mikoto|ja|若多祁命|12=draft}}, whose son Tasanouji founded the Kusakabe-no-Muraji of {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国造|12=draft}}. # Progeny of {{ill|Amaterasu no Mikoto|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft}}, represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫|12=draft}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|author-link=Ota Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans, Volume 2|publisher=Surnames Publishing|year=1934|url={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|id={{NDLBibID|47004572}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kusakabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[ja:日下部氏]] [[sn:Kusakabe clan]] [[en:Kusakabe clan]] [[sn:usakabe clan]]

Lineage The Kusakabe Clan is thought to originate from several genealogical lines: # {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}}'s grandson, {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命}}, a member of the {{ill|Mononobe Clan|ja|物部氏}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|ja|火闌降命}}, regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hayato Clan|ja|隼人}} in {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|Satsuma Province}}, sharing origins with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the {{ill|Star River Rebellion|ja|星川皇子の乱|1=Prince Hoshikawa Rebellion}} under {{ill|Yamato Sumeragi|ja|雄略天皇}}, granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Progeny of {{ill|Prince Ōkusaka|ja|大草香皇子}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s grandson, Omoteomi no Miko (表米親王), who led the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, son of Himusubi no Mikoto, who initiated the Kusakabe-no-Muraji lineage. # Descendants of {{ill|Watake no Mikoto|ja|若多祁命}}, whose son Tasanouji founded the Kusakabe-no-Muraji of {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国造}}. # Progeny of {{ill|Amaterasu no Mikoto|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. 海江田氏?（武家） など }} '''日下部氏'''（くさかべうじ）は、[[日本]]の[[氏族]]のひとつ。家紋は[[木瓜紋|三ッ盛木瓜]]、[[蔦]]、[[三つ銀杏]]。 == 起源 == ;地域 「くさかべ」は[[部民制]]や[[朝臣|朝臣姓]]の氏のひとつであり、日部、＃部（＃は「日」の下に「下」）、日下部、草香部、草部、草加部、草下部、草壁、苅部、早部、久坂部、などの表記がある。名前の分布は[[日向国]]、 [[淡路国]]、[[丹国]]、[[和泉国]]、[[陸奥国]]、[[大宰府]]日下部氏、[[豊後国]]日下部連、[[筑後国]]草壁氏、[[河内国]]草刈部氏（[[尾張国]]日下部氏の祖先と言われる）、[[伊予国]]草部氏など、[[九州|筑紫島]]から[[東北地方]]に渡り、[[犬養部]]の氏も同祖であるとされている{{sfn|太田|1934|p=2026}}。 ;起源 起源にはいくつかの系統がある。 # [[饒速日命]]の孫・[[彦湯支命]]（比古由支命）の後裔。[[物部氏]]の一族。 # [[火闌降命]]（[[薩摩国]]の[[隼人]]族の祖）の後裔で、[[犬養部]]と同祖の氏。 # [[開化天皇]]の皇子[[彦坐王|日子坐]]王の後裔。[[因幡国]]で朝臣姓「[[草香部]]」を名乗った{{sfn|太田|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|[[日本書紀]]によれば、草香部漢彦は[[雄略天皇]]23年、[[星川皇子の乱]]の制圧の際、[[大伴室屋]]に河内[[三野小根]]の救済を願って聞き届けられたといわれる{{sfn|太田|1934|p=2024}}。{{コトバンク|草香部漢彦}}。{{コトバンク|難波日香蚊}}。。}}。 # [[開化天皇]]の孫・[[山代之大筒木真若王]]（[[彦坐王]]の子）に始まる、[[但馬国|但遅麻国造]]族の日下部君（『[[古事記]]』、『[[大日本史]]』）。 # 開化天皇の孫・[[狭穂彦王]]（彦坐王の子）に始まる、[[甲斐国造]]族の日下部直、河内の日下部連。 #[[仁徳天皇]]の皇子である大草香（おおくさか）皇子の後。 # [[孝徳天皇]]の孫・表米親王（[[日下部表米]]）に始まる、日下部宿禰の後裔（『{{ws|[[:s:朝倉始末記|朝倉始末記]]}}』）{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2024}}。 # [[大彦命]]の子・紐結命に始まる日下連。 # 若多祁命の子・田狭乃直に始まる[[伊豆国造]]族の日下部直。 # [[天日和伎命]]の6世孫・[[保都祢命]]の末裔の日下部首。 3・4・5の系統はいずれも彦坐王の後裔とされている。また山代之大筒木真若王後裔説と孝徳天皇後裔説のどちらも表米以降の系譜はほぼ同じであるが、部民制度の成立を考えた場合にとくに7の孝徳天皇後裔説は疑問点が多いとされる{{Sfn|太田|1963|p={{要ページ番号|date=2019年1月}}}}。 また、[[雄略天皇]]の[[皇后]]であり[[仁徳天皇]]の[[皇女]]でもある'''[[草香幡梭姫皇女|草香幡梭姫]]'''が生活する資用に充てられた料地の管理等に携わった[[部民]]が、この[[皇后]]の名に因む（いわゆる[[名代]]部）とする説がある。そして、この部民は各地に配置されて[[屯田兵]]のような軍事集団の性格を持つものでもあったとされる。 '''くさかべ'''（日下部）の表記は、和歌の[[枕詞]]、「日の下の草香（ひのもと の くさか）」より生じた表記と言われる。同様の表記には「長谷の泊瀬」、「春日の滓鹿」、「飛ぶ鳥の明日香」などがある。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} ; {{Notelist}} ;出典 {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Citation|和書|last=太田|first=亮|author-link=太田亮|others=[[上田萬年]]、[[三上参次]]監修|chapter=日下部　クサカベ|pages=2017-2027|year=1934|title=姓氏家系大辞典 第2|publisher=姓氏家系大辞典刊行会|id={{全国書誌番号|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/102}} |volume-title=国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション|ref=harv}} {{フリーアクセス}} * {{Citation|和書|last=太田|first=亮|others=上田萬年、三上参次監修|volume=|date=1963-11|title=姓氏家系大辞典|publisher=[[角川書店]]|ncid=BN01488361|ref=harv}} {{japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くさかへし}} [[Category:日下部氏|!]] [[Category:氏]] [[Category:日本の氏族]] [[Category:近畿地方の氏族]]

(Family Crest of the Tajima Kusakabe Clan) | surname = | founder = | origin = | notable_ancestor = [[Hikozahiko|Hikozahiko]], [[Saho Hiko]], [[Hikoyuki no Mikoto]], Prince Hyōmai? | type = [[Imperial clan]], [[Divine clan]] | location = | notable_members = | descendants = [[Ōtagaki Clan]] (Samurai), [[Yagi Clan]] (Samurai), [[Asakura Clan]] (Samurai), [[Hashimoto Clan]] (Samurai), [[Iwakiri Clan]] (Samurai), [[Kisaichi Clan]] (Samurai), Kusaka Clan? （但馬日下部氏の{{ill|Family Crest|ja|家紋}}） |氏姓 = |始祖 = |出自 = |氏祖 = {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}
{{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}
{{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命}}
表米親王? |種別 = {{ill|Imperial Descent|ja|皇別}}、{{ill|Kami Descent|ja|神別}} |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Otagaki Clan|ja|太田垣氏}}（{{ill|Samurai|ja|武家}}）
{{ill|Yagi Clan|ja|八木氏}}（武家）
{{ill|Asakura Clan|ja|朝倉氏}}（武家）
{{ill|Hashimoto Clan|ja|橋本氏}}（武家）
{{ill|Iwakiri Clan|ja|岩切氏}}（武家）
{{ill|Kisaichi Clan|ja|私市氏}}（武家）
日下氏?（武家）
海江田氏?（武家） など }} '''Kusakabe Clan''' (日下部氏, Kusakabe-uji) is a [[Japanese clan]] with its origins in ancient Japan. Their family crests include {{ill|Wood Sorrel Crest|ja|木瓜紋}}, {{ill|Ivy Crest|ja|蔦}}, and {{ill|Three Ginkgo Crest|ja|三つ銀杏}}. == Origins == ; Region "Kusakabe" is one of the clans associated with the [[Bemin|Be-system]] and noble families holding the {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}} title. Their names appear in various forms, such as 日部, ＃部 (where ＃ combines "日" above "下"), 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, and 久坂部. Their distribution spans regions such as {{ill|Hyūga Province|ja|日向国}}, {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国}}, {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹国}}, {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}, {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province}}, and others. The {{ill|Kusakabe Clan|ja|日下部氏}} of Dazaifu, Kusakabe-no-Muraji in Bungo Province, Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Chikugo Province, Kusakabe Clan of Kawachi Province, and Kusakabe Clan of Iyo Province share a common ancestor with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ; Lineage The Kusakabe Clan is thought to originate from several genealogical lines: # {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}}'s grandson, {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命}}, a member of the {{ill|Mononobe Clan|ja|物部氏}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|ja|火闌降命}}, regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hayato Clan|ja|隼人}} in {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|Satsuma Province}}, sharing origins with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the {{ill|Star River Rebellion|ja|星川皇子の乱|1=Prince Hoshikawa Rebellion}} under {{ill|Yamato Sumeragi|ja|雄略天皇}}, granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Progeny of {{ill|Prince Ōkusaka|ja|大草香皇子}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s grandson, Omoteomi no Miko (表米親王), who led the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, son of Himusubi no Mikoto, who initiated the Kusakabe-no-Muraji lineage. # Descendants of {{ill|Watake no Mikoto|ja|若多祁命}}, whose son Tasanouji founded the Kusakabe-no-Muraji of {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国造}}. # Progeny of {{ill|Amaterasu no Mikoto|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|author-link=Ota Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans, Volume 2|publisher=Surnames Publishing|year=1934|url={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|id={{NDLBibID|47004572}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kusakabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[ja:日下部氏]] [[sn:Kusakabe clan]] [[en:Kusakabe clan]] [[sn:usakabe clan]] {{translated page|ja|日下部氏|version=99796174|comment=Imported full ja history}}

海江田氏?（武家） など }} '''Kusakabe Clan''' (日下部氏, Kusakabe-uji) is a [[Japanese clan]] with its origins in ancient Japan. Their family crests include {{ill|Wood Sorrel Crest|ja|木瓜紋|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ivy Crest|ja|蔦|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Three Ginkgo Crest|ja|三つ銀杏|12=draft}}. == Origins == ; Region "Kusakabe" is one of the clans associated with the [[Bemin|Be-system]] and noble families holding the {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣|12=draft}} title. Their names appear in various forms, such as 日部, ＃部 (where ＃ combines "日" above "下"), 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, and 久坂部. Their distribution spans regions such as {{ill|Hyūga Province|ja|日向国|12=draft}}, {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹国|12=draft}}, {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province|12=draft}}, and others. The {{ill|Kusakabe Clan|ja|日下部氏|12=draft}} of Dazaifu, Kusakabe-no-Muraji in Bungo Province, Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Chikugo Province, Kusakabe Clan of Kawachi Province, and Kusakabe Clan of Iyo Province share a common ancestor with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部|12=draft}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ; Lineage The Kusakabe Clan is thought to originate from several genealogical lines: # {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=draft}}'s grandson, {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命|12=draft}}, a member of the {{ill|Mononobe Clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|ja|火闌降命|12=draft}}, regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hayato Clan|ja|隼人|12=draft}} in {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|Satsuma Province|12=draft}}, sharing origins with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部|12=draft}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=draft}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|12=draft}}, who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=draft}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the {{ill|Star River Rebellion|ja|星川皇子の乱|1=Prince Hoshikawa Rebellion|12=draft}} under {{ill|Yamato Sumeragi|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft}}, granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王|12=draft}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造|12=draft}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=draft}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Progeny of {{ill|Prince Ōkusaka|ja|大草香皇子|12=draft}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|12=draft}}'s grandson, Omoteomi no Miko (表米親王), who led the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|12=draft}}, son of Himusubi no Mikoto, who initiated the Kusakabe-no-Muraji lineage. # Descendants of {{ill|Watake no Mikoto|ja|若多祁命|12=draft}}, whose son Tasanouji founded the Kusakabe-no-Muraji of {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国造|12=draft}}. # Progeny of {{ill|Amaterasu no Mikoto|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft}}, represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫|12=draft}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|author-link=Ota Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans, Volume 2|publisher=Surnames Publishing|year=1934|url={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|id={{NDLBibID|47004572}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|日下部氏|version=99796174|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Kusakabe clan]] [[ja:日下部氏]] [[en:Kusakabe clan]] [[sn:usakabe clan]]

{{ill|Yagi Clan|ja|八木氏|12=draft}}（武家）
{{ill|Asakura Clan|ja|朝倉氏|12=draft}}（武家）

{{ill|Iwakiri Clan|ja|岩切氏|12=draft}}（武家）

日下氏?（武家）
海江田氏?（武家） など }} '''Kusakabe Clan''' (日下部氏, Kusakabe-uji) is a [[Japanese clan]] with its origins in ancient Japan. Their family crests include {{ill|Wood Sorrel Crest|ja|木瓜紋|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ivy Crest|ja|蔦|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Three Ginkgo Crest|ja|三つ銀杏|12=draft}}. == Origins == ; Region "Kusakabe" is one of the clans associated with the [[Bemin|Be-system]] and noble families holding the {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣|12=draft}} title. Their names appear in various forms, such as 日部, ＃部 (where ＃ combines "日" above "下"), 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, and 久坂部. Their distribution spans regions such as {{ill|Hyūga Province|ja|日向国|12=draft}}, {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹国|12=draft}}, {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province|12=draft}}, and others. The {{ill|Kusakabe Clan|ja|日下部氏|12=draft}} of Dazaifu, Kusakabe-no-Muraji in Bungo Province, Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Chikugo Province, Kusakabe Clan of Kawachi Province, and Kusakabe Clan of Iyo Province share a common ancestor with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部|12=draft}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ; Lineage The Kusakabe Clan is thought to originate from several genealogical lines: # {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=draft}}'s grandson, {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命|12=draft}}, a member of the {{ill|Mononobe Clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|ja|火闌降命|12=draft}}, regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hayato Clan|ja|隼人|12=draft}} in {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|Satsuma Province|12=draft}}, sharing origins with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部|12=draft}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=draft}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|12=draft}}, who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=draft}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the {{ill|Star River Rebellion|ja|星川皇子の乱|1=Prince Hoshikawa Rebellion|12=draft}} under {{ill|Yamato Sumeragi|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft}}, granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王|12=draft}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造|12=draft}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=draft}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Progeny of {{ill|Prince Ōkusaka|ja|大草香皇子|12=draft}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|12=draft}}'s grandson, Omoteomi no Miko (表米親王), who led the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|12=draft}}, son of Himusubi no Mikoto, who initiated the Kusakabe-no-Muraji lineage. # Descendants of {{ill|Watake no Mikoto|ja|若多祁命|12=draft}}, whose son Tasanouji founded the Kusakabe-no-Muraji of {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国造|12=draft}}. # Progeny of {{ill|Amaterasu no Mikoto|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft}}, represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫|12=draft}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|author-link=Ota Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans, Volume 2|publisher=Surnames Publishing|year=1934|url={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|id={{NDLBibID|47004572}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} {{translated page|ja|日下部氏|version=99796174|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Kusakabe clan]] [[ja:日下部氏]] [[en:Kusakabe clan]] [[sn:usakabe clan]]

Their distribution spans regions such as {{ill|Hyūga Province|ja|日向国|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹国|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province|qq=draft}}, and others. The {{ill|Kusakabe Clan|ja|日下部氏|qq=draft}} of Dazaifu, Kusakabe-no-Muraji in Bungo Province, Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Chikugo Province, Kusakabe Clan of Kawachi Province, and Kusakabe Clan of Iyo Province share a common ancestor with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部|qq=draft}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ; Lineage The Kusakabe Clan is thought to originate from several genealogical lines: # {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft}}'s grandson, {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命|qq=draft}}, a member of the {{ill|Mononobe Clan|ja|物部氏|qq=draft}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|ja|火闌降命|qq=draft}}, regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hayato Clan|ja|隼人|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|Satsuma Province|qq=draft}}, sharing origins with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部|qq=draft}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|qq=draft}}, who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|qq=draft}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft}}, Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the {{ill|Star River Rebellion|ja|星川皇子の乱|1=Prince Hoshikawa Rebellion|qq=draft}} under {{ill|Yamato Sumeragi|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft}}, granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王|qq=draft}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造|qq=draft}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Progeny of {{ill|Prince Ōkusaka|ja|大草香皇子|qq=draft}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|qq=draft}}'s grandson, Omoteomi no Miko (表米親王), who led the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|qq=draft}}, son of Himusubi no Mikoto, who initiated the Kusakabe-no-Muraji lineage. # Descendants of {{ill|Watake no Mikoto|ja|若多祁命|qq=draft}}, whose son Tasanouji founded the Kusakabe-no-Muraji of {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国造|qq=draft}}. # Progeny of {{ill|Amaterasu no Mikoto|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft}}, represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫|qq=draft}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|author-link=Ota Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans, Volume 2|publisher=Surnames Publishing|year=1934|url={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|id={{NDLBibID|47004572}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} [[Category:translated pages without meta categories]]\n{{translated page|ja|日下部氏|version=99796174|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Kusakabe clan]] [[ja:日下部氏]] [[en:Kusakabe clan]] [[sn:usakabe clan]]

(Samurai), Kaieda Clan? (Samurai), among others. |氏名 = 日下部氏 |image = [[File:Japanese Crest mitumori mokkou.svg|150px]]

表米親王? |種別 = {{ill|Imperial Descent|ja|皇別|qq=draft}}、{{ill|Kami Descent|ja|神別|qq=draft}} |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Otagaki Clan|ja|太田垣氏|qq=draft}}（{{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|qq=draft}}）
{{ill|Yagi Clan|ja|八木氏|qq=draft}}（武家）
{{ill|Asakura Clan|ja|朝倉氏|qq=draft}}（武家）
{{ill|Hashimoto Clan|ja|橋本氏|qq=draft}}（武家）
{{ill|Iwakiri Clan|ja|岩切氏|qq=draft}}（武家）
{{ill|Kisaichi Clan|ja|私市氏|qq=draft}}（武家）
日下氏?（武家）
海江田氏?（武家） など }} '''Kusakabe Clan''' (日下部氏, Kusakabe-uji) is a [[Japanese clan]] with its origins in ancient Japan. Their family crests include {{ill|Wood Sorrel Crest|ja|木瓜紋|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ivy Crest|ja|蔦|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Three Ginkgo Crest|ja|三つ銀杏|qq=draft}}. == Origins == ; Region "Kusakabe" is one of the clans associated with the [[Bemin|Be-system]] and noble families holding the {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣|qq=draft}} title. Their names appear in various forms, such as 日部, ＃部 (where ＃ combines "日" above "下"), 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, and 久坂部. Their distribution spans regions such as {{ill|Hyūga Province|ja|日向国|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹国|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province|qq=draft}}, and others. The {{ill|Kusakabe Clan|ja|日下部氏|qq=draft}} of Dazaifu, Kusakabe-no-Muraji in Bungo Province, Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Chikugo Province, Kusakabe Clan of Kawachi Province, and Kusakabe Clan of Iyo Province share a common ancestor with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部|qq=draft}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ; Lineage The Kusakabe Clan is thought to originate from several genealogical lines: # {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft}}'s grandson, {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命|qq=draft}}, a member of the {{ill|Mononobe Clan|ja|物部氏|qq=draft}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|ja|火闌降命|qq=draft}}, regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hayato Clan|ja|隼人|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|Satsuma Province|qq=draft}}, sharing origins with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部|qq=draft}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|qq=draft}}, who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|qq=draft}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft}}, Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the {{ill|Star River Rebellion|ja|星川皇子の乱|1=Prince Hoshikawa Rebellion|qq=draft}} under {{ill|Yamato Sumeragi|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft}}, granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王|qq=draft}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造|qq=draft}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Progeny of {{ill|Prince Ōkusaka|ja|大草香皇子|qq=draft}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|qq=draft}}'s grandson, Omoteomi no Miko (表米親王), who led the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|qq=draft}}, son of Himusubi no Mikoto, who initiated the Kusakabe-no-Muraji lineage. # Descendants of {{ill|Watake no Mikoto|ja|若多祁命|qq=draft}}, whose son Tasanouji founded the Kusakabe-no-Muraji of {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国造|qq=draft}}. # Progeny of {{ill|Amaterasu no Mikoto|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft}}, represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫|qq=draft}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|author-link=Ota Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans, Volume 2|publisher=Surnames Publishing|year=1934|url={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|id={{NDLBibID|47004572}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} [[Category:translated pages without meta categories]]\n{{translated page|ja|日下部氏|version=99796174|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Kusakabe clan]] [[ja:日下部氏]] [[en:Kusakabe clan]] [[sn:usakabe clan]]

Their distribution spans regions such as {{ill|Hyūga Province|ja|日向国|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹国|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province|qq=draft}}, and others. The {{ill|Kusakabe Clan|ja|日下部氏|qq=draft}} of Dazaifu, Kusakabe-no-Muraji in Bungo Province, Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Chikugo Province, Kusakabe Clan of Kawachi Province, and Kusakabe Clan of Iyo Province share a common ancestor with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部|qq=draft}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ; Lineage The Kusakabe Clan is thought to originate from several genealogical lines: # {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft}}'s grandson, {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命|qq=draft}}, a member of the {{ill|Mononobe Clan|ja|物部氏|qq=draft}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|ja|火闌降命|qq=draft}}, regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hayato Clan|ja|隼人|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|Satsuma Province|qq=draft}}, sharing origins with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部|qq=draft}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|qq=draft}}, who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|qq=draft}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft}}, Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the {{ill|Star River Rebellion|ja|星川皇子の乱|1=Prince Hoshikawa Rebellion|qq=draft}} under {{ill|Yamato Sumeragi|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft}}, granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王|qq=draft}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造|qq=draft}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Progeny of {{ill|Prince Ōkusaka|ja|大草香皇子|qq=draft}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|qq=draft}}'s grandson, Omoteomi no Miko (表米親王), who led the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|qq=draft}}, son of Himusubi no Mikoto, who initiated the Kusakabe-no-Muraji lineage. # Descendants of {{ill|Watake no Mikoto|ja|若多祁命|qq=draft}}, whose son Tasanouji founded the Kusakabe-no-Muraji of {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国造|qq=draft}}. # Progeny of {{ill|Amaterasu no Mikoto|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft}}, represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫|qq=draft}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|author-link=Ota Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans, Volume 2|publisher=Surnames Publishing|year=1934|url={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|id={{NDLBibID|47004572}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|日下部氏|version=99796174|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Kusakabe clan]] [[ja:日下部氏]] [[en:Kusakabe clan]] [[sn:usakabe clan]]

(Samurai), Kaieda Clan? (Samurai), among others. |氏名 = 日下部氏 |image = [[File:Japanese Crest mitumori mokkou.svg|150px]]
みつもりもっこう
{{ill|Wood Sorrel Crest|ja|木瓜紋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood Sorrel Crest|qq=}}
（但馬日下部氏の{{ill|Family Crest|ja|家紋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Family Crest|qq=}}） |氏姓 = |始祖 = |出自 = |氏祖 = {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho-hiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|qq=}}
表米親王? |種別 = {{ill|Imperial Descent|ja|皇別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Descent|qq=}}、{{ill|Kami Descent|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Descent|qq=}} |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Otagaki Clan|ja|太田垣氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otagaki Clan|qq=}}（{{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|qq=}}）
{{ill|Yagi Clan|ja|八木氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Clan|qq=}}（武家）
{{ill|Asakura Clan|ja|朝倉氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakura Clan|qq=}}（武家）
{{ill|Hashimoto Clan|ja|橋本氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto Clan|qq=}}（武家）
{{ill|Iwakiri Clan|ja|岩切氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwakiri Clan|qq=}}（武家）
{{ill|Kisaichi Clan|ja|私市氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisaichi Clan|qq=}}（武家）
日下氏?（武家）
海江田氏?（武家） など }} '''Kusakabe Clan''' (日下部氏, Kusakabe-uji) is a [[Japanese clan]] with its origins in ancient Japan. Their family crests include {{ill|Wood Sorrel Crest|ja|木瓜紋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood Sorrel Crest|qq=}}, {{ill|Ivy Crest|ja|蔦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ivy Crest|qq=}}, and {{ill|Three Ginkgo Crest|ja|三つ銀杏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Ginkgo Crest|qq=}}. == Origins == ; Region "Kusakabe" is one of the clans associated with the [[Bemin|Be-system]] and noble families holding the {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asomi|qq=}} title. Their names appear in various forms, such as 日部, ＃部 (where ＃ combines "日" above "下"), 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, and 久坂部. Their distribution spans regions such as {{ill|Hyūga Province|ja|日向国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyūga Province|qq=}}, {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awaji Province|qq=}}, {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}}, {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Province|qq=}}, {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province|qq=}}, and others. The {{ill|Kusakabe Clan|ja|日下部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe Clan|qq=}} of Dazaifu, Kusakabe-no-Muraji in Bungo Province, Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Chikugo Province, Kusakabe Clan of Kawachi Province, and Kusakabe Clan of Iyo Province share a common ancestor with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inukai Clan|qq=}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ; Lineage The Kusakabe Clan is thought to originate from several genealogical lines: # {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi no Mikoto|qq=}}'s grandson, {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|qq=}}, a member of the {{ill|Mononobe Clan|ja|物部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Clan|qq=}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|ja|火闌降命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|qq=}}, regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hayato Clan|ja|隼人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato Clan|qq=}} in {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|Satsuma Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsuma Province|qq=}}, sharing origins with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inukai Clan|qq=}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kaika|qq=}}, who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|qq=}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the {{ill|Star River Rebellion|ja|星川皇子の乱|1=Prince Hoshikawa Rebellion|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star River Rebellion|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Hoshikawa Rebellion|qq=}} under {{ill|Yamato Sumeragi|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Sumeragi|qq=}}, granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|qq=}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|qq=}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho-hiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Progeny of {{ill|Prince Ōkusaka|ja|大草香皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōkusaka|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōtoku|qq=}}'s grandson, Omoteomi no Miko (表米親王), who led the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, son of Himusubi no Mikoto, who initiated the Kusakabe-no-Muraji lineage. # Descendants of {{ill|Watake no Mikoto|ja|若多祁命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watake no Mikoto|qq=}}, whose son Tasanouji founded the Kusakabe-no-Muraji of {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu Province|qq=}}. # Progeny of {{ill|Amaterasu no Mikoto|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}, represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|qq=}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|author-link=Ota Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans, Volume 2|publisher=Surnames Publishing|year=1934|url={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|id={{NDLBibID|47004572}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|日下部氏|version=99796174|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Kusakabe clan]] [[ja:日下部氏]] [[en:Kusakabe clan]] [[sn:usakabe clan]]

Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|qq=}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|qq=}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho-hiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Progeny of {{ill|Prince Ōkusaka|ja|大草香皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōkusaka|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōtoku|qq=}}'s grandson, Omoteomi no Miko (表米親王), who led the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, son of Himusubi no Mikoto, who initiated the Kusakabe-no-Muraji lineage. # Descendants of {{ill|Watake no Mikoto|ja|若多祁命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watake no Mikoto|qq=}}, whose son Tasanouji founded the Kusakabe-no-Muraji of {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu Province|qq=}}. # Progeny of {{ill|Amaterasu no Mikoto|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}, represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|qq=}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|author-link=Ota Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans, Volume 2|publisher=Surnames Publishing|year=1934|url={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|id={{NDLBibID|47004572}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|日下部氏|version=99796174|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Kusakabe clan]] [[ja:日下部氏]] [[en:Kusakabe clan]] [[sn:usakabe clan]] ==Merged second translation== '''Kusakabe clan (Hyūga Province)''' {{Confuse|Kusakabe clan (Hyūga Province)|Kusakabe}} {{Infobox Japanese clans (Ancient Clans) |name = Kusakabe Clan (日下部氏) |image = [[File:Japanese Crest mitumori mokkou.svg|150px]]
Mitsumori Mokko
[[Mokko mon|Mitsumori Mokko]]
(Crest of the Tajima Kusakabe Clan) |surname = |founder = |origin = |ancestor = [[Hikozakaru]]
[[Sahohiko no Mikoto]]
[[Hikoyukushi no Mikoto]]
Omotoyone no Ō? |type = [[Kōbetsu]] (Imperial lineage), [[Shinbetsu]] (Divine lineage) |main_branch = |notable_members = |descendants = [[Otagaki clan]] (Samurai)
[[Yagi clan]] (Samurai)
[[Asakura clan]] (Samurai)
[[Hashimoto clan]] (Samurai)
[[Iwakiri clan]] (Samurai)
[[Kisai clan]] (Samurai)
Kusaka clan? (Samurai)
Kaieda clan? (Samurai), etc. }} The '''Kusakabe clan''' (日下部氏) was a Japanese clan. Their family crests (kamon) included the [[Mokko mon|Mitsumori Mokko]], [[Tsuba (plant)|Ivy]], and [[Mitsui Ginkgo]]. == Origins == ;Geographical Distribution The name "Kusakabe" was a part of the [[Tomobe system]] and an appellation used by the '''Asomi'''-Ranked clans. It had various written forms such as 日部, ⿰日下, 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, 久坂部, etc. The clan was dispersed across regions like [[Hyūga Province]], [[Awaji Province]], [[Tango Province]], [[Izumi Province]], [[Mutsu Province]], and more, including Dazaifu's Kusakabe clan, the Kusakabe no Muraji of [[Bungo Province]], the Kusakabe no Muraji of [[Chikugo Province]], the Kusakari clan of [[Kawachi Province]] (believed to be an ancestor of the Kusakabe clan of [[Owari Province]]), and the Kusabe clan of [[Iyo Province]]. The clan shared ancestry with the [[Inukai clan]].{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ;Lineage There are multiple proposed lineages of the Kusakabe clan: # Descendants of [[Hikoyukushi no Mikoto]], grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]], making them a branch of the [[Mononobe clan]]. # A lineage from [[Hi no Morihiko no Mikoto]] (the progenitor of the [[Satsuma Province]]'s [[Hayato people]]), sharing ancestry with the [[Inukai clan]]. # Descendants of [[Hikozakaru no Mikoto]], son of [[Emperor Kaika]], who later became known as "Kusakabe no Asomi" in [[Inaba Province]].{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the [[Hoshikawa Rebellion]] during Emperor Yūryaku’s reign.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Kotobank|草香部漢彦}}.{{Kotobank|難波日香蚊}}..}} # A lineage from [[Yamashiro no Ōtsutsuki no Mawakao]], son of Emperor Kaika, leading to the Kusakabe no Kimi of the [[Tajima Province|Tajima no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] clan (recorded in the [[Kojiki]] and [[Dai Nihonshi]]). # Descendants of [[Sahohiko no Mikoto]], son of Emperor Kaika, leading to the Kusakabe no Ason of [[Kai Province]] and the Kusakabe no Muraji of Kawachi. # A lineage from Prince Ōkusaka, son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]. # A lineage from [[Emperor Kōtoku]]'s grandson, Omotoyone no Ō (also known as Kusakabe no Omotoyone), leading to the Kusakabe no Sukune clan (mentioned in the {{ws|[[:s:朝倉始末記|Asakura Shimatsuki]]}}).{{Sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of [[Ōhiko no Mikoto]]'s son, Himusubi no Mikoto, forming the Kusaka no Muraji lineage. # Descendants of Wakakake no Mikoto's son, Tasano no Ason, forming the Kusakabe no Ason lineage in [[Izu Province]]. # A lineage from [[Ame-no-Hiyori no Mikoto]]'s sixth-generation descendant, [[Hoto Ne no Mikoto]], forming the Kusakabe no Omi clan. Lineages 3, 4, and 5 all claim descent from Hikozakaru no Mikoto. Additionally, the lineage from Yamashiro no Ōtsutsuki no Mawakao and the one from Emperor Kōtoku share nearly identical genealogies after Omotoyone. However, given the structure of the [[Tomobe system]], the claim of descent from Emperor Kōtoku is often considered questionable.{{Sfn|Ota|1963|p={{Need page number|date=January 2019}}}} Another theory suggests that the Kusakabe clan originated as an administrative unit managing lands assigned for '''Princess Kusaka-hata-no-misuhime''' (草香幡梭姫), the consort of [[Emperor Yūryaku]] and daughter of [[Emperor Nintoku]]. This theory aligns with the existence of '''Nashiro''' (名代) groups, which were placed throughout the country and may have also served as local military units akin to [[tondenhei]]. The term '''Kusakabe''' (日下部) is thought to have derived from the poetic phrase "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香) in waka poetry, similar to expressions like "Hase no Tomari," "Kasuga no Kagatsuki," and "Tobu Tori no Asuka." == References == {{reflist}} == Notes == {{notelist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|ja|last=Ota|first=Ryo|author-link=Ota Ryo|others=[[Ueda Mannen]], [[Mikami Sanji]] (eds.)|chapter=Kusakabe|pages=2017-2027|year=1934|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Vol.2|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kankōkai|id={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|volume-title=National Diet Library Digital Collection|ref=harv}} {{Free access}} * {{Citation|ja|last=Ota|first=Ryo|others=Ueda Mannen, Mikami Sanji (eds.)|date=November 1963|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=[[Kadokawa Shoten]]|ncid=BN01488361|ref=harv}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|日下部氏|version=99796174|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kusakabe clan (Hyūga Province)]] [[Category:Kusakabe clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kinki region clans]] }} [[ja:%E6%97%A5%E4%B8%8B%E9%83%A8%E6%B0%8F]] [[sn:Kusakabe clan (Hyūga Province)]] [[sn:usakabe clan (Hyūga Province)]] [[ja:日下部氏]] [[Category:Merged pages]]

海江田氏?（武家） など }} '''Kusakabe Clan''' (日下部氏, Kusakabe-uji) is a [[Japanese clan]] with its origins in ancient Japan. Their family crests include {{ill|Wood Sorrel Crest|ja|木瓜紋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood Sorrel Crest|qq=}}, {{ill|Ivy Crest|ja|蔦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ivy Crest|qq=}}, and {{ill|Three Ginkgo Crest|ja|三つ銀杏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Ginkgo Crest|qq=}}. == Origins == ; Region "Kusakabe" is one of the clans associated with the [[Bemin|Be-system]] and noble families holding the {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asomi|qq=}} title. Their names appear in various forms, such as 日部, ＃部 (where ＃ combines "日" above "下"), 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, and 久坂部. Their distribution spans regions such as {{ill|Hyūga Province|ja|日向国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyūga Province|qq=}}, {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awaji Province|qq=}}, {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}}, {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Province|qq=}}, {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province|qq=}}, and others. The {{ill|Kusakabe Clan|ja|日下部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe Clan|qq=}} of Dazaifu, Kusakabe-no-Muraji in Bungo Province, Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Chikugo Province, Kusakabe Clan of Kawachi Province, and Kusakabe Clan of Iyo Province share a common ancestor with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inukai Clan|qq=}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ; Lineage The Kusakabe Clan is thought to originate from several genealogical lines: # {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi no Mikoto|qq=}}'s grandson, {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|qq=}}, a member of the {{ill|Mononobe Clan|ja|物部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Clan|qq=}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|ja|火闌降命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|qq=}}, regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hayato Clan|ja|隼人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato Clan|qq=}} in {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|Satsuma Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsuma Province|qq=}}, sharing origins with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inukai Clan|qq=}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kaika|qq=}}, who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|qq=}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the {{ill|Star River Rebellion|ja|星川皇子の乱|1=Prince Hoshikawa Rebellion|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star River Rebellion|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Hoshikawa Rebellion|qq=}} under {{ill|Yamato Sumeragi|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Sumeragi|qq=}}, granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|qq=}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|qq=}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho-hiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Progeny of {{ill|Prince Ōkusaka|ja|大草香皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōkusaka|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōtoku|qq=}}'s grandson, Omoteomi no Miko (表米親王), who led the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, son of Himusubi no Mikoto, who initiated the Kusakabe-no-Muraji lineage. # Descendants of {{ill|Watake no Mikoto|ja|若多祁命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watake no Mikoto|qq=}}, whose son Tasanouji founded the Kusakabe-no-Muraji of {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu Province|qq=}}. # Progeny of {{ill|Amaterasu no Mikoto|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}, represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|qq=}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|author-link=Ota Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans, Volume 2|publisher=Surnames Publishing|year=1934|url={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|id={{NDLBibID|47004572}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|日下部氏|version=99796174|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Kusakabe clan]] [[ja:日下部氏]] [[en:Kusakabe clan]] [[sn:usakabe clan]] ==Merged second translation== '''Kusakabe clan (Hyūga Province)''' {{Confuse|Kusakabe clan (Hyūga Province)|Kusakabe}} {{Infobox Japanese clans (Ancient Clans) |name = Kusakabe Clan (日下部氏) |image = [[File:Japanese Crest mitumori mokkou.svg|150px]]

Kaieda clan? (Samurai), etc. }} The '''Kusakabe clan''' (日下部氏) was a Japanese clan. Their family crests (kamon) included the [[Mokko mon|Mitsumori Mokko]], [[Tsuba (plant)|Ivy]], and [[Mitsui Ginkgo]]. == Origins == ;Geographical Distribution The name "Kusakabe" was a part of the [[Tomobe system]] and an appellation used by the '''Asomi'''-Ranked clans. It had various written forms such as 日部, ⿰日下, 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, 久坂部, etc. The clan was dispersed across regions like [[Hyūga Province]], [[Awaji Province]], [[Tango Province]], [[Izumi Province]], [[Mutsu Province]], and more, including Dazaifu's Kusakabe clan, the Kusakabe no Muraji of [[Bungo Province]], the Kusakabe no Muraji of [[Chikugo Province]], the Kusakari clan of [[Kawachi Province]] (believed to be an ancestor of the Kusakabe clan of [[Owari Province]]), and the Kusabe clan of [[Iyo Province]]. The clan shared ancestry with the [[Inukai clan]].{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ;Lineage There are multiple proposed lineages of the Kusakabe clan: # Descendants of [[Hikoyukushi no Mikoto]], grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]], making them a branch of the [[Mononobe clan]]. # A lineage from [[Hi no Morihiko no Mikoto]] (the progenitor of the [[Satsuma Province]]'s [[Hayato people]]), sharing ancestry with the [[Inukai clan]]. # Descendants of [[Hikozakaru no Mikoto]], son of [[Emperor Kaika]], who later became known as "Kusakabe no Asomi" in [[Inaba Province]].{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the [[Hoshikawa Rebellion]] during Emperor Yūryaku’s reign.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Kotobank|草香部漢彦}}.{{Kotobank|難波日香蚊}}..}} # A lineage from [[Yamashiro no Ōtsutsuki no Mawakao]], son of Emperor Kaika, leading to the Kusakabe no Kimi of the [[Tajima Province|Tajima no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] clan (recorded in the [[Kojiki]] and [[Dai Nihonshi]]). # Descendants of [[Sahohiko no Mikoto]], son of Emperor Kaika, leading to the Kusakabe no Ason of [[Kai Province]] and the Kusakabe no Muraji of Kawachi. # A lineage from Prince Ōkusaka, son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]. # A lineage from [[Emperor Kōtoku]]'s grandson, Omotoyone no Ō (also known as Kusakabe no Omotoyone), leading to the Kusakabe no Sukune clan (mentioned in the {{ws|[[:s:朝倉始末記|Asakura Shimatsuki]]}}).{{Sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of [[Ōhiko-no-Mikoto]]'s son, Himusubi no Mikoto, forming the Kusaka no Muraji lineage. # Descendants of Wakakake no Mikoto's son, Tasano no Ason, forming the Kusakabe no Ason lineage in [[Izu Province]]. # A lineage from [[Ame-no-Hiyori no Mikoto]]'s sixth-generation descendant, [[Hoto Ne no Mikoto]], forming the Kusakabe no Omi clan. Lineages 3, 4, and 5 all claim descent from Hikozakaru no Mikoto. Additionally, the lineage from Yamashiro no Ōtsutsuki no Mawakao and the one from Emperor Kōtoku share nearly identical genealogies after Omotoyone. However, given the structure of the [[Tomobe system]], the claim of descent from Emperor Kōtoku is often considered questionable.{{Sfn|Ota|1963|p={{Need page number|date=January 2019}}}} Another theory suggests that the Kusakabe clan originated as an administrative unit managing lands assigned for '''Princess Kusaka-hata-no-misuhime''' (草香幡梭姫), the consort of [[Emperor Yūryaku]] and daughter of [[Emperor Nintoku]]. This theory aligns with the existence of '''Nashiro''' (名代) groups, which were placed throughout the country and may have also served as local military units akin to [[tondenhei]]. The term '''Kusakabe''' (日下部) is thought to have derived from the poetic phrase "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香) in waka poetry, similar to expressions like "Hase no Tomari," "Kasuga no Kagatsuki," and "Tobu Tori no Asuka." == References == {{reflist}} == Notes == {{notelist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|ja|last=Ota|first=Ryo|author-link=Ota Ryo|others=[[Ueda Mannen]], [[Mikami Sanji]] (eds.)|chapter=Kusakabe|pages=2017-2027|year=1934|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Vol.2|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kankōkai|id={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|volume-title=National Diet Library Digital Collection|ref=harv}} {{Free access}} * {{Citation|ja|last=Ota|first=Ryo|others=Ueda Mannen, Mikami Sanji (eds.)|date=November 1963|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=[[Kadokawa Shoten]]|ncid=BN01488361|ref=harv}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|日下部氏|version=99796174|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kusakabe clan (Hyūga Province)]] [[Category:Kusakabe clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kinki region clans]] }} [[ja:%E6%97%A5%E4%B8%8B%E9%83%A8%E6%B0%8F]] [[sn:Kusakabe clan (Hyūga Province)]] [[sn:usakabe clan (Hyūga Province)]] [[ja:日下部氏]] [[Category:Merged pages]]
