{{ill|Yagi Clan|ja|八木氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Clan|qq=}}（武家）
[[:en:Asakura clan|Asakura Clan]]（武家）
{{ill|Hashimoto Clan|ja|橋本氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto Clan|qq=}}（武家）
{{ill|Iwakiri Clan|ja|岩切氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwakiri Clan|qq=}}（武家）
{{ill|Kisaichi Clan|ja|私市氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisaichi Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}（武家）
日下氏?（武家）
海江田氏?（武家） など }} '''Kusakabe Clan''' (日下部氏, Kusakabe-uji) is a [[Japanese clans|Japanese clan]] with its origins in ancient Japan. Their family crests include {{ill|Wood Sorrel Crest|ja|木瓜紋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood Sorrel Crest|qq=}}, {{ill|Ivy Crest|ja|蔦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ivy Crest|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツタ}}, and {{ill|Three Ginkgo Crest|ja|三つ銀杏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Ginkgo Crest|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Origins == ; Region "Kusakabe" is one of the clans associated with the [[:en:Bemin|Be-system]] and noble families holding the [[:en:Ason|Asomi]] title. Their names appear in various forms, such as 日部, ＃部 (where ＃ combines "日" above "下"), 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, and 久坂部. Their distribution spans regions such as [[:en:Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]], [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}}, [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]], [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]], and others. The {{ill|Kusakabe Clan|ja|日下部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe Clan|qq=}} of Dazaifu, Kusakabe-no-Muraji in Bungo Province, Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Chikugo Province, Kusakabe Clan of Kawachi Province, and Kusakabe Clan of Iyo Province share a common ancestor with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inukai Clan|qq=}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ; Lineage The Kusakabe Clan is thought to originate from several genealogical lines: # {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}'s grandson, {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|qq=}}, a member of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]]. # Descendants of {{ill|Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|ja|火闌降命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火須勢理命}}, regarded as the progenitor of the [[:en:Hayato people|Hayato Clan]] in {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|Satsuma Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsuma Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, sharing origins with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inukai Clan|qq=}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the [[:en:Prince Hoshikawa Rebellion|Prince Hoshikawa Rebellion]] under [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Yamato Sumeragi]], granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|qq=}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho-hiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Progeny of {{ill|Prince Ōkusaka|ja|大草香皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōkusaka|qq=}}, son of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. # Descendants of [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]]'s grandson, Omoteomi no Miko (表米親王), who led the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, son of Himusubi no Mikoto, who initiated the Kusakabe-no-Muraji lineage. # Descendants of {{ill|Watake no Mikoto|ja|若多祁命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, whose son Tasanouji founded the Kusakabe-no-Muraji of {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu Province|qq=}}. # Progeny of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|author-link=Ota Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans, Volume 2|publisher=Surnames Publishing|year=1934|url={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|id={{NDLBibID|47004572}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|日下部氏|version=99796174|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Kusakabe clan]] [[ja:日下部氏]] [[sn:usakabe clan]] ==Merged second translation== '''Kusakabe clan (Hyūga Province)''' {{Confuse|Kusakabe clan (Hyūga Province)|Kusakabe}} {{Infobox Japanese clans (Ancient Clans) |name = Kusakabe Clan (日下部氏) |image = [[File:Japanese Crest mitumori mokkou.svg|150px]]
Mitsumori Mokko
[[Mokko mon|Mitsumori Mokko]]
(Crest of the Tajima Kusakabe Clan) |surname = |founder = |origin = |ancestor = [[Hikozakaru]]
[[Sahohiko no Mikoto]]
[[Hikoyukushi no Mikoto]]
Omotoyone no Ō? |type = [[Kōbetsu]] (Imperial lineage), [[Shinbetsu]] (Divine lineage) |main_branch = |notable_members = |descendants = [[Otagaki clan]] (Samurai)
[[Yagi clan]] (Samurai)
[[Asakura clan]] (Samurai)
[[Hashimoto clan]] (Samurai)
[[Iwakiri clan]] (Samurai)
[[Kisai clan]] (Samurai)
Kusaka clan? (Samurai)
Kaieda clan? (Samurai), etc. }} The '''Kusakabe clan''' (日下部氏) was a Japanese clan. Their family crests (kamon) included the [[Mokko mon|Mitsumori Mokko]], [[Tsuba (plant)|Ivy]], and [[Mitsui Ginkgo]]. == Origins == ;Geographical Distribution The name "Kusakabe" was a part of the [[Tomobe system]] and an appellation used by the '''Asomi'''-Ranked clans. It had various written forms such as 日部, ⿰日下, 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, 久坂部, etc. The clan was dispersed across regions like [[:en:Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]], [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], [[Tango Province]], [[Izumi Province]], [[Mutsu Province]], and more, including Dazaifu's Kusakabe clan, the Kusakabe no Muraji of [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], the Kusakabe no Muraji of [[:en:Chikugo Province|Chikugo Province]], the Kusakari clan of [[Kawachi Province]] (believed to be an ancestor of the Kusakabe clan of [[Owari Province]]), and the Kusabe clan of [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]]. The clan shared ancestry with the [[Agata no Inukai clan]].{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ;Lineage There are multiple proposed lineages of the Kusakabe clan: # Descendants of [[Hikoyukushi no Mikoto]], grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]], making them a branch of the [[Mononobe clan]]. # A lineage from [[Hi no Morihiko no Mikoto]] (the progenitor of the [[Satsuma Province]]'s [[:en:Hayato people|Hayato people]]), sharing ancestry with the [[Agata no Inukai clan]]. # Descendants of [[Hikozakaru no Mikoto]], son of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], who later became known as "Kusakabe no Asomi" in [[:en:Inaba Province|Inaba Province]].{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the [[Hoshikawa Rebellion]] during Emperor Yūryaku’s reign.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Kotobank|草香部漢彦}}.{{Kotobank|難波日香蚊}}..}} # A lineage from [[Yamashiro no Ōtsutsuki no Mawakao]], son of Emperor Kaika, leading to the Kusakabe no Kimi of the [[Tajima Province|Tajima no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] clan (recorded in the [[Kojiki]] and [[:en:Dai Nihonshi|Dai Nihonshi]]). # Descendants of [[Sahohiko no Mikoto]], son of Emperor Kaika, leading to the Kusakabe no Ason of [[Kai Province]] and the Kusakabe no Muraji of Kawachi. # A lineage from Prince Ōkusaka, son of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. # A lineage from [[Emperor Kōtoku]]'s grandson, Omotoyone no Ō (also known as Kusakabe no Omotoyone), leading to the Kusakabe no Sukune clan (mentioned in the {{ws|[[:s:朝倉始末記|Asakura Shimatsuki]]}}).{{Sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of [[Ōhiko-no-Mikoto]]'s son, Himusubi no Mikoto, forming the Kusaka no Muraji lineage. # Descendants of Wakakake no Mikoto's son, Tasano no Ason, forming the Kusakabe no Ason lineage in [[:en:Izu Province|Izu Province]]. # A lineage from [[Ame-no-Hiyori no Mikoto]]'s sixth-generation descendant, [[Hoto Ne no Mikoto]], forming the Kusakabe no Omi clan. Lineages 3, 4, and 5 all claim descent from Hikozakaru no Mikoto. Additionally, the lineage from Yamashiro no Ōtsutsuki no Mawakao and the one from Emperor Kōtoku share nearly identical genealogies after Omotoyone. However, given the structure of the [[Tomobe system]], the claim of descent from Emperor Kōtoku is often considered questionable.{{Sfn|Ota|1963|p={{Need page number|date=January 2019}}}} Another theory suggests that the Kusakabe clan originated as an administrative unit managing lands assigned for '''Princess Kusaka-hata-no-misuhime''' (草香幡梭姫), the consort of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]] and daughter of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. This theory aligns with the existence of '''Nashiro''' (名代) groups, which were placed throughout the country and may have also served as local military units akin to [[tondenhei]]. The term '''Kusakabe''' (日下部) is thought to have derived from the poetic phrase "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香) in waka poetry, similar to expressions like "Hase no Tomari," "Kasuga no Kagatsuki," and "Tobu Tori no Asuka." == References == {{reflist}} == Notes == {{notelist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|ja|last=Ota|first=Ryo|author-link=Ota Ryo|others=[[Ueda Mannen]], [[Mikami Sanji]] (eds.)|chapter=Kusakabe|pages=2017-2027|year=1934|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Vol.2|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kankōkai|id={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|volume-title=National Diet Library Digital Collection|ref=harv}} {{Free access}} * {{Citation|ja|last=Ota|first=Ryo|others=Ueda Mannen, Mikami Sanji (eds.)|date=November 1963|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=[[Kadokawa Shoten]]|ncid=BN01488361|ref=harv}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|日下部氏|version=99796174|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] }} [[ja:%E6%97%A5%E4%B8%8B%E9%83%A8%E6%B0%8F]] [[sn:Kusakabe clan (Hyūga Province)]] [[sn:usakabe clan (Hyūga Province)]] [[ja:日下部氏]] [[sn:Kusakabe clan]] [[sn:Kusakabe clan]] [[Category:日下部氏]] [[Category:氏]] [[Category:近畿地方の氏族]]

Their family crests include {{ill|Wood Sorrel Crest|ja|木瓜紋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood Sorrel Crest|qq=}}, {{ill|Ivy Crest|ja|蔦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ivy Crest|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツタ}}, and {{ill|Three Ginkgo Crest|ja|三つ銀杏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Ginkgo Crest|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Origins == ; Region "Kusakabe" is one of the clans associated with the [[:en:Bemin|Be-system]] and noble families holding the [[:en:Ason|Asomi]] title. Their names appear in various forms, such as 日部, ＃部 (where ＃ combines "日" above "下"), 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, and 久坂部. Their distribution spans regions such as [[:en:Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]], [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}}, [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]], [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]], and others. The {{ill|Kusakabe Clan|ja|日下部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe Clan|qq=}} of Dazaifu, Kusakabe-no-Muraji in Bungo Province, Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Chikugo Province, Kusakabe Clan of Kawachi Province, and Kusakabe Clan of Iyo Province share a common ancestor with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inukai Clan|qq=}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ; Lineage The Kusakabe Clan is thought to originate from several genealogical lines: # {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}'s grandson, {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|qq=}}, a member of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]]. # Descendants of {{ill|Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|ja|火闌降命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火須勢理命}}, regarded as the progenitor of the [[:en:Hayato people|Hayato Clan]] in {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|Satsuma Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsuma Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, sharing origins with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inukai Clan|qq=}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the [[:en:Prince Hoshikawa Rebellion|Prince Hoshikawa Rebellion]] under [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Yamato Sumeragi]], granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Progeny of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|author-link=Ota Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans, Volume 2|publisher=Surnames Publishing|year=1934|url={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|id={{NDLBibID|47004572}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|日下部氏|version=99796174|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Kusakabe clan]] [[ja:日下部氏]] [[sn:usakabe clan]] ==Merged second translation== '''Kusakabe clan (Hyūga Province)''' {{Confuse|Kusakabe clan (Hyūga Province)|Kusakabe}} {{Infobox Japanese clans (Ancient Clans) |name = Kusakabe Clan (日下部氏) |image = [[File:Japanese Crest mitumori mokkou.svg|150px]]
Mitsumori Mokko
[[Mokko mon|Mitsumori Mokko]]
(Crest of the Tajima Kusakabe Clan) |surname = |founder = |origin = |ancestor = [[Hikozakaru]]
[[Sahohiko no Mikoto]]
[[Hikoyukushi no Mikoto]]
Omotoyone no Ō? |type = [[Kōbetsu]] (Imperial lineage), [[Shinbetsu]] (Divine lineage) |main_branch = |notable_members = |descendants = [[Otagaki clan]] (Samurai)
[[Yagi clan]] (Samurai)
[[Asakura clan]] (Samurai)
[[Hashimoto clan]] (Samurai)
[[Iwakiri clan]] (Samurai)
[[Kisai clan]] (Samurai)
Kusaka clan? (Samurai)
Kaieda clan? (Samurai), etc. }} The '''Kusakabe clan''' (日下部氏) was a Japanese clan. Their family crests (kamon) included the [[Mokko mon|Mitsumori Mokko]], [[Tsuba (plant)|Ivy]], and [[Mitsui Ginkgo]]. == Origins == ;Geographical Distribution The name "Kusakabe" was a part of the [[Tomobe system]] and an appellation used by the '''Asomi'''-Ranked clans. It had various written forms such as 日部, ⿰日下, 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, 久坂部, etc. The clan was dispersed across regions like [[:en:Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]], [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], [[Tango Province]], [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]], [[Mutsu Province]], and more, including Dazaifu's Kusakabe clan, the Kusakabe no Muraji of [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], the Kusakabe no Muraji of [[:en:Chikugo Province|Chikugo Province]], the Kusakari clan of [[Kawachi Province]] (believed to be an ancestor of the Kusakabe clan of [[Owari Province]]), and the Kusabe clan of [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]]. The clan shared ancestry with the [[Agata no Inukai clan]].{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ;Lineage There are multiple proposed lineages of the Kusakabe clan: # Descendants of [[Hikoyukushi no Mikoto]], grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]], making them a branch of the [[Mononobe clan]]. # A lineage from [[Hi no Morihiko no Mikoto]] (the progenitor of the [[Satsuma Province]]'s [[:en:Hayato people|Hayato people]]), sharing ancestry with the [[Agata no Inukai clan]]. # Descendants of [[Hikozakaru no Mikoto]], son of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], who later became known as "Kusakabe no Asomi" in [[:en:Inaba Province|Inaba Province]].{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the [[Hoshikawa Rebellion]] during Emperor Yūryaku’s reign.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Kotobank|草香部漢彦}}.{{Kotobank|難波日香蚊}}..}} # A lineage from [[Yamashiro no Ōtsutsuki no Mawakao]], son of Emperor Kaika, leading to the Kusakabe no Kimi of the [[Tajima Province|Tajima no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] clan (recorded in the [[Kojiki]] and [[:en:Dai Nihonshi|Dai Nihonshi]]). # Descendants of [[Sahohiko no Mikoto]], son of Emperor Kaika, leading to the Kusakabe no Ason of [[Kai Province]] and the Kusakabe no Muraji of Kawachi. # A lineage from Prince Ōkusaka, son of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. # A lineage from [[Emperor Kōtoku]]'s grandson, Omotoyone no Ō (also known as Kusakabe no Omotoyone), leading to the Kusakabe no Sukune clan (mentioned in the {{ws|[[:s:朝倉始末記|Asakura Shimatsuki]]}}).{{Sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of [[Ōhiko-no-Mikoto]]'s son, Himusubi no Mikoto, forming the Kusaka no Muraji lineage. # Descendants of Wakakake no Mikoto's son, Tasano no Ason, forming the Kusakabe no Ason lineage in [[:en:Izu Province|Izu Province]]. # A lineage from [[Ame-no-Hiyori no Mikoto]]'s sixth-generation descendant, [[Hoto Ne no Mikoto]], forming the Kusakabe no Omi clan. Lineages 3, 4, and 5 all claim descent from Hikozakaru no Mikoto. Additionally, the lineage from Yamashiro no Ōtsutsuki no Mawakao and the one from Emperor Kōtoku share nearly identical genealogies after Omotoyone. However, given the structure of the [[Tomobe system]], the claim of descent from Emperor Kōtoku is often considered questionable.{{Sfn|Ota|1963|p={{Need page number|date=January 2019}}}} Another theory suggests that the Kusakabe clan originated as an administrative unit managing lands assigned for '''Princess Kusaka-hata-no-misuhime''' (草香幡梭姫), the consort of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]] and daughter of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. This theory aligns with the existence of '''Nashiro''' (名代) groups, which were placed throughout the country and may have also served as local military units akin to [[tondenhei]]. The term '''Kusakabe''' (日下部) is thought to have derived from the poetic phrase "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香) in waka poetry, similar to expressions like "Hase no Tomari," "Kasuga no Kagatsuki," and "Tobu Tori no Asuka." == References == {{reflist}} == Notes == {{notelist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|ja|last=Ota|first=Ryo|author-link=Ota Ryo|others=[[Ueda Mannen]], [[Mikami Sanji]] (eds.)|chapter=Kusakabe|pages=2017-2027|year=1934|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Vol.2|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kankōkai|id={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|volume-title=National Diet Library Digital Collection|ref=harv}} {{Free access}} * {{Citation|ja|last=Ota|first=Ryo|others=Ueda Mannen, Mikami Sanji (eds.)|date=November 1963|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=[[Kadokawa Shoten]]|ncid=BN01488361|ref=harv}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|日下部氏|version=99796174|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] }} [[ja:%E6%97%A5%E4%B8%8B%E9%83%A8%E6%B0%8F]] [[sn:Kusakabe clan (Hyūga Province)]] [[sn:usakabe clan (Hyūga Province)]] [[ja:日下部氏]] [[sn:Kusakabe clan]] [[sn:Kusakabe clan]] [[Category:日下部氏]] [[Category:氏]] [[Category:近畿地方の氏族]]

{{Confusion|日下部氏 (日向国)|日下部}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | clan_name = Kusakabe Clan (日下部氏) | image = [[File:Japanese Crest mitumori mokkou.svg|150px]]
Mitsumori Mokkou
[[Mokkou mon|Three-Tiered Mokkou]]
(Family Crest of the Tajima Kusakabe Clan) | surname = | founder = | origin = | notable_ancestor = [[Hikozahiko|Hikozahiko]], [[Saho Hiko]], [[Hikoyuki no Mikoto]], Prince Hyōmai? | type = [[Imperial clan]], [[Divine clan]] | location = | notable_members = | descendants = [[Ōtagaki Clan]] (Samurai), [[Yagi Clan]] (Samurai), [[Asakura Clan]] (Samurai), [[Hashimoto Clan]] (Samurai), [[Iwakiri Clan]] (Samurai), [[Kisaichi Clan]] (Samurai), Kusaka Clan? (Samurai), Kaieda Clan? (Samurai), among others. |氏名 = 日下部氏 |image = [[File:Japanese Crest mitumori mokkou.svg|150px]]
みつもりもっこう
{{ill|Wood Sorrel Crest|ja|木瓜紋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood Sorrel Crest|qq=}}
（但馬日下部氏の[[:en:Mon (emblem)|Family Crest]]） |氏姓 = |始祖 = |出自 = |氏祖 = {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho-hiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|qq=}}
表米親王? |種別 = {{ill|Imperial Descent|ja|皇別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Descent|qq=}}、{{ill|Kami Descent|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Descent|qq=}} |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Otagaki Clan|ja|太田垣氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otagaki Clan|qq=}}（{{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}）
{{ill|Yagi Clan|ja|八木氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Clan|qq=}}（武家）
[[:en:Asakura clan|Asakura Clan]]（武家）
{{ill|Hashimoto Clan|ja|橋本氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto Clan|qq=}}（武家）
{{ill|Iwakiri Clan|ja|岩切氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwakiri Clan|qq=}}（武家）
{{ill|Kisaichi Clan|ja|私市氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisaichi Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}（武家）
日下氏?（武家）
海江田氏?（武家） など }} '''Kusakabe Clan''' (日下部氏, Kusakabe-uji) is a [[Japanese clans|Japanese clan]] with its origins in ancient Japan. Their family crests include {{ill|Wood Sorrel Crest|ja|木瓜紋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood Sorrel Crest|qq=}}, {{ill|Ivy Crest|ja|蔦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ivy Crest|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツタ}}, and {{ill|Three Ginkgo Crest|ja|三つ銀杏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Ginkgo Crest|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Origins == ; Region "Kusakabe" is one of the clans associated with the [[:en:Bemin|Be-system]] and noble families holding the [[:en:Ason|Asomi]] title. Their names appear in various forms, such as 日部, ＃部 (where ＃ combines "日" above "下"), 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, and 久坂部. Their distribution spans regions such as [[:en:Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]], [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}}, [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]], [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]], and others. The {{ill|Kusakabe Clan|ja|日下部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe Clan|qq=}} of Dazaifu, Kusakabe-no-Muraji in Bungo Province, Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Chikugo Province, Kusakabe Clan of Kawachi Province, and Kusakabe Clan of Iyo Province share a common ancestor with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inukai Clan|qq=}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ; Lineage The Kusakabe Clan is thought to originate from several genealogical lines: # {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}'s grandson, {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|qq=}}, a member of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]]. # Descendants of {{ill|Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|ja|火闌降命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火須勢理命}}, regarded as the progenitor of the [[:en:Hayato people|Hayato Clan]] in {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|Satsuma Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsuma Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, sharing origins with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inukai Clan|qq=}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the [[:en:Prince Hoshikawa Rebellion|Prince Hoshikawa Rebellion]] under [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Yamato Sumeragi]], granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|qq=}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho-hiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Progeny of {{ill|Prince Ōkusaka|ja|大草香皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōkusaka|qq=}}, son of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. # Descendants of [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]]'s grandson, Omoteomi no Miko (表米親王), who led the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, son of Himusubi no Mikoto, who initiated the Kusakabe-no-Muraji lineage. # Descendants of {{ill|Watake no Mikoto|ja|若多祁命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, whose son Tasanouji founded the Kusakabe-no-Muraji of {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu Province|qq=}}. # Progeny of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|author-link=Ota Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans, Volume 2|publisher=Surnames Publishing|year=1934|url={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|id={{NDLBibID|47004572}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|日下部氏|version=99796174|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Kusakabe clan]] [[ja:日下部氏]] [[sn:usakabe clan]] ==Merged second translation== '''Kusakabe clan (Hyūga Province)''' {{Confuse|Kusakabe clan (Hyūga Province)|Kusakabe}} {{Infobox Japanese clans (Ancient Clans) |name = Kusakabe Clan (日下部氏) |image = [[File:Japanese Crest mitumori mokkou.svg|150px]]
Mitsumori Mokko
[[Mokko mon|Mitsumori Mokko]]

Kaieda clan? (Samurai), etc. }} The '''Kusakabe clan''' (日下部氏) was a Japanese clan. Their family crests (kamon) included the [[Mokko mon|Mitsumori Mokko]], [[Tsuba (plant)|Ivy]], and [[Mitsui Ginkgo]]. == Origins == ;Geographical Distribution The name "Kusakabe" was a part of the [[Tomobe system]] and an appellation used by the '''Asomi'''-Ranked clans. It had various written forms such as 日部, ⿰日下, 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, 久坂部, etc. The clan was dispersed across regions like [[:en:Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]], [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], [[Tango Province]], [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]], [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]], and more, including Dazaifu's Kusakabe clan, the Kusakabe no Muraji of [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], the Kusakabe no Muraji of [[:en:Chikugo Province|Chikugo Province]], the Kusakari clan of [[Kawachi Province]] (believed to be an ancestor of the Kusakabe clan of [[Owari Province]]), and the Kusabe clan of [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]]. The clan shared ancestry with the [[Agata no Inukai clan]].{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ;Lineage There are multiple proposed lineages of the Kusakabe clan: # Descendants of [[Hikoyukushi no Mikoto]], grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]], making them a branch of the [[Mononobe clan]]. # A lineage from [[Hi no Morihiko no Mikoto]] (the progenitor of the [[Satsuma Province]]'s [[:en:Hayato people|Hayato people]]), sharing ancestry with the [[Agata no Inukai clan]]. # Descendants of [[Hikozakaru no Mikoto]], son of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], who later became known as "Kusakabe no Asomi" in [[:en:Inaba Province|Inaba Province]].{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the [[Hoshikawa Rebellion]] during Emperor Yūryaku’s reign.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Kotobank|草香部漢彦}}.{{Kotobank|難波日香蚊}}..}} # A lineage from [[Yamashiro no Ōtsutsuki no Mawakao]], son of Emperor Kaika, leading to the Kusakabe no Kimi of the [[Tajima Province|Tajima no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] clan (recorded in the [[Kojiki]] and [[:en:Dai Nihonshi|Dai Nihonshi]]). # Descendants of [[Sahohiko no Mikoto]], son of Emperor Kaika, leading to the Kusakabe no Ason of [[Kai Province]] and the Kusakabe no Muraji of Kawachi. # A lineage from Prince Ōkusaka, son of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. # A lineage from [[Emperor Kōtoku]]'s grandson, Omotoyone no Ō (also known as Kusakabe no Omotoyone), leading to the Kusakabe no Sukune clan (mentioned in the {{ws|[[:s:朝倉始末記|Asakura Shimatsuki]]}}).{{Sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of [[Ōhiko-no-Mikoto]]'s son, Himusubi no Mikoto, forming the Kusaka no Muraji lineage. # Descendants of Wakakake no Mikoto's son, Tasano no Ason, forming the Kusakabe no Ason lineage in [[:en:Izu Province|Izu Province]]. # A lineage from [[Ame-no-Hiyori no Mikoto]]'s sixth-generation descendant, [[Hoto Ne no Mikoto]], forming the Kusakabe no Omi clan. Lineages 3, 4, and 5 all claim descent from Hikozakaru no Mikoto. Additionally, the lineage from Yamashiro no Ōtsutsuki no Mawakao and the one from Emperor Kōtoku share nearly identical genealogies after Omotoyone. However, given the structure of the [[Tomobe system]], the claim of descent from Emperor Kōtoku is often considered questionable.{{Sfn|Ota|1963|p={{Need page number|date=January 2019}}}} Another theory suggests that the Kusakabe clan originated as an administrative unit managing lands assigned for '''Princess Kusaka-hata-no-misuhime''' (草香幡梭姫), the consort of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]] and daughter of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. This theory aligns with the existence of '''Nashiro''' (名代) groups, which were placed throughout the country and may have also served as local military units akin to [[tondenhei]]. The term '''Kusakabe''' (日下部) is thought to have derived from the poetic phrase "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香) in waka poetry, similar to expressions like "Hase no Tomari," "Kasuga no Kagatsuki," and "Tobu Tori no Asuka." == References == {{reflist}} == Notes == {{notelist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|ja|last=Ota|first=Ryo|author-link=Ota Ryo|others=[[Ueda Mannen]], [[Mikami Sanji]] (eds.)|chapter=Kusakabe|pages=2017-2027|year=1934|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Vol.2|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kankōkai|id={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|volume-title=National Diet Library Digital Collection|ref=harv}} {{Free access}} * {{Citation|ja|last=Ota|first=Ryo|others=Ueda Mannen, Mikami Sanji (eds.)|date=November 1963|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=[[Kadokawa Shoten]]|ncid=BN01488361|ref=harv}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|日下部氏|version=99796174|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] }} [[ja:%E6%97%A5%E4%B8%8B%E9%83%A8%E6%B0%8F]] [[sn:Kusakabe clan (Hyūga Province)]] [[sn:usakabe clan (Hyūga Province)]] [[ja:日下部氏]] [[sn:Kusakabe clan]] [[sn:Kusakabe clan]] [[Category:日下部氏]] [[Category:氏]] [[Category:近畿地方の氏族]]

（但馬日下部氏の[[:en:Mon (emblem)|Family Crest]]） |氏姓 = |始祖 = |出自 = |氏祖 = {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho-hiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}

{{ill|Yagi Clan|ja|八木氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Clan|qq=}}（武家）
[[:en:Asakura clan|Asakura Clan]]（武家）
{{ill|Hashimoto Clan|ja|橋本氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto Clan|qq=}}（武家）
{{ill|Iwakiri Clan|ja|岩切氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwakiri Clan|qq=}}（武家）
{{ill|Kisaichi Clan|ja|私市氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisaichi Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}（武家）
日下氏?（武家）
海江田氏?（武家） など }} '''Kusakabe Clan''' (日下部氏, Kusakabe-uji) is a [[Japanese clans|Japanese clan]] with its origins in ancient Japan. Their family crests include {{ill|Wood Sorrel Crest|ja|木瓜紋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood Sorrel Crest|qq=}}, {{ill|Ivy Crest|ja|蔦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ivy Crest|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツタ}}, and {{ill|Three Ginkgo Crest|ja|三つ銀杏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Ginkgo Crest|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Origins == ; Region "Kusakabe" is one of the clans associated with the [[:en:Bemin|Be-system]] and noble families holding the [[:en:Ason|Asomi]] title. Their names appear in various forms, such as 日部, ＃部 (where ＃ combines "日" above "下"), 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, and 久坂部. Their distribution spans regions such as [[:en:Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]], [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}}, [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]], [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]], and others. The {{ill|Kusakabe Clan|ja|日下部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe Clan|qq=}} of Dazaifu, Kusakabe-no-Muraji in Bungo Province, Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Chikugo Province, Kusakabe Clan of Kawachi Province, and Kusakabe Clan of Iyo Province share a common ancestor with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inukai Clan|qq=}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ; Lineage The Kusakabe Clan is thought to originate from several genealogical lines: # {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}'s grandson, {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|qq=}}, a member of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]]. # Descendants of {{ill|Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|ja|火闌降命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火須勢理命}}, regarded as the progenitor of the [[:en:Hayato people|Hayato Clan]] in {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|Satsuma Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsuma Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, sharing origins with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inukai Clan|qq=}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the [[:en:Prince Hoshikawa Rebellion|Prince Hoshikawa Rebellion]] under [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Yamato Sumeragi]], granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|qq=}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho-hiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Progeny of {{ill|Prince Ōkusaka|ja|大草香皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōkusaka|qq=}}, son of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. # Descendants of [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]]'s grandson, Omoteomi no Miko (表米親王), who led the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, son of Himusubi no Mikoto, who initiated the Kusakabe-no-Muraji lineage. # Descendants of {{ill|Watake no Mikoto|ja|若多祁命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, whose son Tasanouji founded the Kusakabe-no-Muraji of {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu Province|qq=}}. # Progeny of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|author-link=Ota Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans, Volume 2|publisher=Surnames Publishing|year=1934|url={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|id={{NDLBibID|47004572}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|日下部氏|version=99796174|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Kusakabe clan]] [[ja:日下部氏]] [[sn:usakabe clan]] ==Merged second translation== '''Kusakabe clan (Hyūga Province)''' {{Confuse|Kusakabe clan (Hyūga Province)|Kusakabe}} {{Infobox Japanese clans (Ancient Clans) |name = Kusakabe Clan (日下部氏) |image = [[File:Japanese Crest mitumori mokkou.svg|150px]]
Mitsumori Mokko
[[Mokko mon|Mitsumori Mokko]]
(Crest of the Tajima Kusakabe Clan) |surname = |founder = |origin = |ancestor = [[Hikozakaru]]
[[Sahohiko no Mikoto]]
[[Hikoyukushi no Mikoto]]
Omotoyone no Ō? |type = [[Kōbetsu]] (Imperial lineage), [[Shinbetsu]] (Divine lineage) |main_branch = |notable_members = |descendants = [[Otagaki clan]] (Samurai)
[[Yagi clan]] (Samurai)
[[Asakura clan]] (Samurai)
[[Hashimoto clan]] (Samurai)
[[Iwakiri clan]] (Samurai)
[[Kisai clan]] (Samurai)
Kusaka clan? (Samurai)
Kaieda clan? (Samurai), etc. }} The '''Kusakabe clan''' (日下部氏) was a Japanese clan. Their family crests (kamon) included the [[Mokko mon|Mitsumori Mokko]], [[Tsuba (plant)|Ivy]], and [[Mitsui Ginkgo]]. == Origins == ;Geographical Distribution The name "Kusakabe" was a part of the [[Tomobe system]] and an appellation used by the '''Asomi'''-Ranked clans. It had various written forms such as 日部, ⿰日下, 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, 久坂部, etc. The clan was dispersed across regions like [[:en:Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]], [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], [[Tango Province]], [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]], [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]], and more, including Dazaifu's Kusakabe clan, the Kusakabe no Muraji of [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], the Kusakabe no Muraji of [[:en:Chikugo Province|Chikugo Province]], the Kusakari clan of [[Kawachi Province]] (believed to be an ancestor of the Kusakabe clan of [[Owari Province]]), and the Kusabe clan of [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]]. The clan shared ancestry with the [[Agata no Inukai clan]].{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ;Lineage There are multiple proposed lineages of the Kusakabe clan: # Descendants of [[Hikoyukushi no Mikoto]], grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]], making them a branch of the [[Mononobe clan]]. # A lineage from [[Hi no Morihiko no Mikoto]] (the progenitor of the [[Satsuma Province]]'s [[:en:Hayato people|Hayato people]]), sharing ancestry with the [[Agata no Inukai clan]]. # Descendants of [[Hikozakaru no Mikoto]], son of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], who later became known as "Kusakabe no Asomi" in [[:en:Inaba Province|Inaba Province]].{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the [[Hoshikawa Rebellion]] during Emperor Yūryaku’s reign.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Kotobank|草香部漢彦}}.{{Kotobank|難波日香蚊}}..}} # A lineage from [[Yamashiro no Ōtsutsuki no Mawakao]], son of Emperor Kaika, leading to the Kusakabe no Kimi of the [[Tajima Province|Tajima no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] clan (recorded in the [[Kojiki]] and [[:en:Dai Nihonshi|Dai Nihonshi]]). # Descendants of [[Sahohiko no Mikoto]], son of Emperor Kaika, leading to the Kusakabe no Ason of [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]] and the Kusakabe no Muraji of Kawachi. # A lineage from Prince Ōkusaka, son of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. # A lineage from [[Emperor Kōtoku]]'s grandson, Omotoyone no Ō (also known as Kusakabe no Omotoyone), leading to the Kusakabe no Sukune clan (mentioned in the {{ws|[[:s:朝倉始末記|Asakura Shimatsuki]]}}).{{Sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of [[Ōhiko-no-Mikoto]]'s son, Himusubi no Mikoto, forming the Kusaka no Muraji lineage. # Descendants of Wakakake no Mikoto's son, Tasano no Ason, forming the Kusakabe no Ason lineage in [[:en:Izu Province|Izu Province]]. # A lineage from [[Ame-no-Hiyori no Mikoto]]'s sixth-generation descendant, [[Hoto Ne no Mikoto]], forming the Kusakabe no Omi clan. Lineages 3, 4, and 5 all claim descent from Hikozakaru no Mikoto. Additionally, the lineage from Yamashiro no Ōtsutsuki no Mawakao and the one from Emperor Kōtoku share nearly identical genealogies after Omotoyone. However, given the structure of the [[Tomobe system]], the claim of descent from Emperor Kōtoku is often considered questionable.{{Sfn|Ota|1963|p={{Need page number|date=January 2019}}}} Another theory suggests that the Kusakabe clan originated as an administrative unit managing lands assigned for '''Princess Kusaka-hata-no-misuhime''' (草香幡梭姫), the consort of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]] and daughter of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. This theory aligns with the existence of '''Nashiro''' (名代) groups, which were placed throughout the country and may have also served as local military units akin to [[tondenhei]]. The term '''Kusakabe''' (日下部) is thought to have derived from the poetic phrase "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香) in waka poetry, similar to expressions like "Hase no Tomari," "Kasuga no Kagatsuki," and "Tobu Tori no Asuka." == References == {{reflist}} == Notes == {{notelist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|ja|last=Ota|first=Ryo|author-link=Ota Ryo|others=[[Ueda Mannen]], [[Mikami Sanji]] (eds.)|chapter=Kusakabe|pages=2017-2027|year=1934|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Vol.2|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kankōkai|id={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|volume-title=National Diet Library Digital Collection|ref=harv}} {{Free access}} * {{Citation|ja|last=Ota|first=Ryo|others=Ueda Mannen, Mikami Sanji (eds.)|date=November 1963|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=[[Kadokawa Shoten]]|ncid=BN01488361|ref=harv}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|日下部氏|version=99796174|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] }} [[ja:%E6%97%A5%E4%B8%8B%E9%83%A8%E6%B0%8F]] [[sn:Kusakabe clan (Hyūga Province)]] [[sn:usakabe clan (Hyūga Province)]] [[ja:日下部氏]] [[sn:Kusakabe clan]] [[sn:Kusakabe clan]] [[Category:日下部氏]] [[Category:氏]] [[Category:近畿地方の氏族]]

海江田氏?（武家） など }} '''Kusakabe Clan''' (日下部氏, Kusakabe-uji) is a [[Japanese clans|Japanese clan]] with its origins in ancient Japan. Their family crests include {{ill|Wood Sorrel Crest|ja|木瓜紋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood Sorrel Crest|qq=}}, {{ill|Ivy Crest|ja|蔦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ivy Crest|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツタ}}, and {{ill|Three Ginkgo Crest|ja|三つ銀杏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Ginkgo Crest|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Origins == ; Region "Kusakabe" is one of the clans associated with the [[:en:Bemin|Be-system]] and noble families holding the [[:en:Ason|Asomi]] title. Their names appear in various forms, such as 日部, ＃部 (where ＃ combines "日" above "下"), 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, and 久坂部. Their distribution spans regions such as [[:en:Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]], [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}}, [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]], [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]], and others. The {{ill|Kusakabe Clan|ja|日下部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe Clan|qq=}} of Dazaifu, Kusakabe-no-Muraji in Bungo Province, Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Chikugo Province, Kusakabe Clan of Kawachi Province, and Kusakabe Clan of Iyo Province share a common ancestor with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inukai Clan|qq=}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ; Lineage The Kusakabe Clan is thought to originate from several genealogical lines: # {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}'s grandson, {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|qq=}}, a member of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]]. # Descendants of {{ill|Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|ja|火闌降命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火須勢理命}}, regarded as the progenitor of the [[:en:Hayato people|Hayato Clan]] in {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|Satsuma Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsuma Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, sharing origins with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inukai Clan|qq=}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the [[:en:Prince Hoshikawa Rebellion|Prince Hoshikawa Rebellion]] under [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Yamato Sumeragi]], granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|qq=}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho-hiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Progeny of {{ill|Prince Ōkusaka|ja|大草香皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōkusaka|qq=}}, son of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. # Descendants of [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]]'s grandson, Omoteomi no Miko (表米親王), who led the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, son of Himusubi no Mikoto, who initiated the Kusakabe-no-Muraji lineage. # Descendants of {{ill|Watake no Mikoto|ja|若多祁命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, whose son Tasanouji founded the Kusakabe-no-Muraji of {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu Province|qq=}}. # Progeny of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|author-link=Ota Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans, Volume 2|publisher=Surnames Publishing|year=1934|url={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|id={{NDLBibID|47004572}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|日下部氏|version=99796174|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Kusakabe clan]] [[ja:日下部氏]] [[sn:usakabe clan]] ==Merged second translation== '''Kusakabe clan (Hyūga Province)''' {{Confuse|Kusakabe clan (Hyūga Province)|Kusakabe}} {{Infobox Japanese clans (Ancient Clans) |name = Kusakabe Clan (日下部氏) |image = [[File:Japanese Crest mitumori mokkou.svg|150px]]
Mitsumori Mokko
[[Mokko mon|Mitsumori Mokko]]
(Crest of the Tajima Kusakabe Clan) |surname = |founder = |origin = |ancestor = [[Hikozakaru]]
[[Sahohiko no Mikoto]]
[[Hikoyukushi no Mikoto]]
Omotoyone no Ō? |type = [[Kōbetsu]] (Imperial lineage), [[Shinbetsu]] (Divine lineage) |main_branch = |notable_members = |descendants = [[Otagaki clan]] (Samurai)
[[Yagi clan]] (Samurai)
[[Asakura clan]] (Samurai)
[[Hashimoto clan]] (Samurai)
[[Iwakiri clan]] (Samurai)
[[Kisai clan]] (Samurai)
Kusaka clan? (Samurai)
Kaieda clan? (Samurai), etc. }} The '''Kusakabe clan''' (日下部氏) was a Japanese clan. Their family crests (kamon) included the [[Mokko mon|Mitsumori Mokko]], [[Tsuba (plant)|Ivy]], and [[Mitsui Ginkgo]]. == Origins == ;Geographical Distribution The name "Kusakabe" was a part of the [[Tomobe system]] and an appellation used by the '''Asomi'''-Ranked clans. It had various written forms such as 日部, ⿰日下, 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, 久坂部, etc. The clan was dispersed across regions like [[:en:Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]], [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], [[Tango Province]], [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]], [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]], and more, including Dazaifu's Kusakabe clan, the Kusakabe no Muraji of [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], the Kusakabe no Muraji of [[:en:Chikugo Province|Chikugo Province]], the Kusakari clan of [[Kawachi Province]] (believed to be an ancestor of the Kusakabe clan of [[Owari Province]]), and the Kusabe clan of [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]]. The clan shared ancestry with the [[Agata no Inukai clan]].{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ;Lineage There are multiple proposed lineages of the Kusakabe clan: # Descendants of [[Hikoyukushi no Mikoto]], grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]], making them a branch of the [[Mononobe clan]]. # A lineage from [[Hi no Morihiko no Mikoto]] (the progenitor of the [[Satsuma Province]]'s [[:en:Hayato people|Hayato people]]), sharing ancestry with the [[Agata no Inukai clan]]. # Descendants of [[Hikozakaru no Mikoto]], son of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], who later became known as "Kusakabe no Asomi" in [[:en:Inaba Province|Inaba Province]].{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the [[Hoshikawa Rebellion]] during Emperor Yūryaku’s reign.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Kotobank|草香部漢彦}}.{{Kotobank|難波日香蚊}}..}} # A lineage from [[Yamashiro no Ōtsutsuki no Mawakao]], son of Emperor Kaika, leading to the Kusakabe no Kimi of the [[Tajima Province|Tajima no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] clan (recorded in the [[Kojiki]] and [[:en:Dai Nihonshi|Dai Nihonshi]]). # Descendants of [[Sahohiko no Mikoto]], son of Emperor Kaika, leading to the Kusakabe no Ason of [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]] and the Kusakabe no Muraji of Kawachi. # A lineage from Prince Ōkusaka, son of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. # A lineage from [[Emperor Kōtoku]]'s grandson, Omotoyone no Ō (also known as Kusakabe no Omotoyone), leading to the Kusakabe no Sukune clan (mentioned in the {{ws|[[:s:朝倉始末記|Asakura Shimatsuki]]}}).{{Sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of [[Ōhiko-no-Mikoto]]'s son, Himusubi no Mikoto, forming the Kusaka no Muraji lineage. # Descendants of Wakakake no Mikoto's son, Tasano no Ason, forming the Kusakabe no Ason lineage in [[:en:Izu Province|Izu Province]]. # A lineage from [[Ame-no-Hiyori no Mikoto]]'s sixth-generation descendant, [[Hoto Ne no Mikoto]], forming the Kusakabe no Omi clan. Lineages 3, 4, and 5 all claim descent from Hikozakaru no Mikoto. Additionally, the lineage from Yamashiro no Ōtsutsuki no Mawakao and the one from Emperor Kōtoku share nearly identical genealogies after Omotoyone. However, given the structure of the [[Tomobe system]], the claim of descent from Emperor Kōtoku is often considered questionable.{{Sfn|Ota|1963|p={{Need page number|date=January 2019}}}} Another theory suggests that the Kusakabe clan originated as an administrative unit managing lands assigned for '''Princess Kusaka-hata-no-misuhime''' (草香幡梭姫), the consort of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]] and daughter of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. This theory aligns with the existence of '''Nashiro''' (名代) groups, which were placed throughout the country and may have also served as local military units akin to [[tondenhei]]. The term '''Kusakabe''' (日下部) is thought to have derived from the poetic phrase "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香) in waka poetry, similar to expressions like "Hase no Tomari," "Kasuga no Kagatsuki," and "Tobu Tori no Asuka." == References == {{reflist}} == Notes == {{notelist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|ja|last=Ota|first=Ryo|author-link=Ota Ryo|others=[[Ueda Mannen]], [[Mikami Sanji]] (eds.)|chapter=Kusakabe|pages=2017-2027|year=1934|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Vol.2|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kankōkai|id={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|volume-title=National Diet Library Digital Collection|ref=harv}} {{Free access}} * {{Citation|ja|last=Ota|first=Ryo|others=Ueda Mannen, Mikami Sanji (eds.)|date=November 1963|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=[[Kadokawa Shoten]]|ncid=BN01488361|ref=harv}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|日下部氏|version=99796174|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] }} [[ja:%E6%97%A5%E4%B8%8B%E9%83%A8%E6%B0%8F]] [[sn:Kusakabe clan (Hyūga Province)]] [[sn:usakabe clan (Hyūga Province)]] [[ja:日下部氏]] [[sn:Kusakabe clan]] [[sn:Kusakabe clan]] [[Category:日下部氏]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:近畿地方の氏族]]

海江田氏?（武家） など }} '''Kusakabe Clan''' (日下部氏, Kusakabe-uji) is a [[Japanese clans|Japanese clan]] with its origins in ancient Japan. Their family crests include {{ill|Wood Sorrel Crest|ja|木瓜紋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood Sorrel Crest|qq=}}, {{ill|Ivy Crest|ja|蔦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ivy Crest|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツタ}}, and {{ill|Three Ginkgo Crest|ja|三つ銀杏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Ginkgo Crest|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Origins == ; Region "Kusakabe" is one of the clans associated with the [[:en:Bemin|Be-system]] and noble families holding the [[:en:Ason|Asomi]] title. Their names appear in various forms, such as 日部, ＃部 (where ＃ combines "日" above "下"), 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, and 久坂部. Their distribution spans regions such as [[:en:Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]], [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}}, [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]], [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]], and others. The {{ill|Kusakabe Clan|ja|日下部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe Clan|qq=}} of Dazaifu, Kusakabe-no-Muraji in Bungo Province, Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Chikugo Province, Kusakabe Clan of Kawachi Province, and Kusakabe Clan of Iyo Province share a common ancestor with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inukai Clan|qq=}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ; Lineage The Kusakabe Clan is thought to originate from several genealogical lines: # {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}'s grandson, {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|qq=}}, a member of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]]. # Descendants of {{ill|Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|ja|火闌降命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火須勢理命}}, regarded as the progenitor of the [[:en:Hayato people|Hayato Clan]] in {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|Satsuma Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsuma Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, sharing origins with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inukai Clan|qq=}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the [[:en:Prince Hoshikawa Rebellion|Prince Hoshikawa Rebellion]] under [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Yamato Sumeragi]], granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|qq=}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho-hiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Progeny of {{ill|Prince Ōkusaka|ja|大草香皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōkusaka|qq=}}, son of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. # Descendants of [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]]'s grandson, Omoteomi no Miko (表米親王), who led the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, son of Himusubi no Mikoto, who initiated the Kusakabe-no-Muraji lineage. # Descendants of {{ill|Watake no Mikoto|ja|若多祁命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, whose son Tasanouji founded the Kusakabe-no-Muraji of {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu Province|qq=}}. # Progeny of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|author-link=Ota Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans, Volume 2|publisher=Surnames Publishing|year=1934|url={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|id={{NDLBibID|47004572}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|日下部氏|version=99796174|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Kusakabe clan]] [[ja:日下部氏]] [[sn:usakabe clan]] ==Merged second translation== '''Kusakabe clan (Hyūga Province)''' {{Confuse|Kusakabe clan (Hyūga Province)|Kusakabe}} {{Infobox Japanese clans (Ancient Clans) |name = Kusakabe Clan (日下部氏) |image = [[File:Japanese Crest mitumori mokkou.svg|150px]]
Mitsumori Mokko

Their family crests (kamon) included the [[Mokko mon|Mitsumori Mokko]], [[Tsuba (plant)|Ivy]], and [[Mitsui Ginkgo]]. == Origins == ;Geographical Distribution The name "Kusakabe" was a part of the [[Tomobe system]] and an appellation used by the '''Asomi'''-Ranked clans. It had various written forms such as 日部, ⿰日下, 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, 久坂部, etc. The clan was dispersed across regions like [[:en:Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]], [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], [[Tango Province]], [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]], [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]], and more, including Dazaifu's Kusakabe clan, the Kusakabe no Muraji of [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], the Kusakabe no Muraji of [[:en:Chikugo Province|Chikugo Province]], the Kusakari clan of [[Kawachi Province]] (believed to be an ancestor of the Kusakabe clan of [[Owari Province]]), and the Kusabe clan of [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]]. The clan shared ancestry with the [[Agata no Inukai clan]].{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ;Lineage There are multiple proposed lineages of the Kusakabe clan: # Descendants of [[Hikoyukushi no Mikoto]], grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]], making them a branch of the [[Mononobe clan]]. # A lineage from [[Hi no Morihiko no Mikoto]] (the progenitor of the [[Satsuma Province]]'s [[:en:Hayato people|Hayato people]]), sharing ancestry with the [[Agata no Inukai clan]]. # Descendants of [[Hikozakaru no Mikoto]], son of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], who later became known as "Kusakabe no Asomi" in [[:en:Inaba Province|Inaba Province]].{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the [[Hoshikawa Rebellion]] during Emperor Yūryaku’s reign.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Kotobank|草香部漢彦}}.{{Kotobank|難波日香蚊}}..}} # A lineage from [[Yamashiro no Ōtsutsuki no Mawakao]], son of Emperor Kaika, leading to the Kusakabe no Kimi of the [[Tajima Province|Tajima no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] clan (recorded in the [[Kojiki]] and [[:en:Dai Nihonshi|Dai Nihonshi]]). # Descendants of [[Sahohiko no Mikoto]], son of Emperor Kaika, leading to the Kusakabe no Ason of [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]] and the Kusakabe no Muraji of Kawachi. # A lineage from Prince Ōkusaka, son of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. # A lineage from [[Emperor Kōtoku]]'s grandson, Omotoyone no Ō (also known as Kusakabe no Omotoyone), leading to the Kusakabe no Sukune clan (mentioned in the {{ws|[[:s:朝倉始末記|Asakura Shimatsuki]]}}).{{Sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of [[Ōhiko-no-Mikoto]]'s son, Himusubi no Mikoto, forming the Kusaka no Muraji lineage. # Descendants of Wakakake no Mikoto's son, Tasano no Ason, forming the Kusakabe no Ason lineage in [[:en:Izu Province|Izu Province]]. # A lineage from [[Ame-no-Hiyori no Mikoto]]'s sixth-generation descendant, [[Hoto Ne no Mikoto]], forming the Kusakabe no Omi clan. Lineages 3, 4, and 5 all claim descent from Hikozakaru no Mikoto. Additionally, the lineage from Yamashiro no Ōtsutsuki no Mawakao and the one from Emperor Kōtoku share nearly identical genealogies after Omotoyone. However, given the structure of the [[Tomobe system]], the claim of descent from Emperor Kōtoku is often considered questionable.{{Sfn|Ota|1963|p={{Need page number|date=January 2019}}}} Another theory suggests that the Kusakabe clan originated as an administrative unit managing lands assigned for '''Princess Kusaka-hata-no-misuhime''' (草香幡梭姫), the consort of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]] and daughter of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. This theory aligns with the existence of '''Nashiro''' (名代) groups, which were placed throughout the country and may have also served as local military units akin to [[tondenhei]]. The term '''Kusakabe''' (日下部) is thought to have derived from the poetic phrase "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香) in waka poetry, similar to expressions like "Hase no Tomari," "Kasuga no Kagatsuki," and "Tobu Tori no Asuka." == References == {{reflist}} == Notes == {{notelist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|ja|last=Ota|first=Ryo|author-link=Ota Ryo|others=[[Ueda Mannen]], [[Mikami Sanji]] (eds.)|chapter=Kusakabe|pages=2017-2027|year=1934|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Vol.2|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kankōkai|id={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|volume-title=National Diet Library Digital Collection|ref=harv}} {{Free access}} * {{Citation|ja|last=Ota|first=Ryo|others=Ueda Mannen, Mikami Sanji (eds.)|date=November 1963|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=[[Kadokawa Shoten]]|ncid=BN01488361|ref=harv}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|日下部氏|version=99796174|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] }} [[ja:%E6%97%A5%E4%B8%8B%E9%83%A8%E6%B0%8F]] [[sn:Kusakabe clan (Hyūga Province)]] [[sn:usakabe clan (Hyūga Province)]] [[ja:日下部氏]] [[sn:Kusakabe clan]] [[sn:Kusakabe clan]] [[Category:日下部氏]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Clans by region]]

(Samurai), Kaieda Clan? (Samurai), among others. |氏名 = 日下部氏 |image = [[File:Japanese Crest mitumori mokkou.svg|150px]]

（但馬日下部氏の[[:en:Mon (emblem)|Family Crest]]） |氏姓 = |始祖 = |出自 = |氏祖 = {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho-hiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|qq=}}
表米親王? |種別 = {{ill|Imperial Descent|ja|皇別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Descent|qq=}}、{{ill|Kami Descent|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Descent|qq=}} |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Otagaki Clan|ja|太田垣氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otagaki Clan|qq=}}（{{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}）
{{ill|Yagi Clan|ja|八木氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Clan|qq=}}（武家）
[[:en:Asakura clan|Asakura Clan]]（武家）
{{ill|Hashimoto Clan|ja|橋本氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto Clan|qq=}}（武家）
{{ill|Iwakiri Clan|ja|岩切氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwakiri Clan|qq=}}（武家）
{{ill|Kisaichi Clan|ja|私市氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisaichi Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}（武家）
日下氏?（武家）
海江田氏?（武家） など }} '''Kusakabe Clan''' (日下部氏, Kusakabe-uji) is a [[Japanese clans|Japanese clan]] with its origins in ancient Japan. Their family crests include {{ill|Wood Sorrel Crest|ja|木瓜紋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood Sorrel Crest|qq=}}, {{ill|Ivy Crest|ja|蔦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ivy Crest|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツタ}}, and {{ill|Three Ginkgo Crest|ja|三つ銀杏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Ginkgo Crest|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Origins == ; Region "Kusakabe" is one of the clans associated with the [[:en:Bemin|Be-system]] and noble families holding the [[:en:Ason|Asomi]] title. Their names appear in various forms, such as 日部, ＃部 (where ＃ combines "日" above "下"), 日下部, 草香部, 草部, 草加部, 草下部, 草壁, 苅部, 早部, and 久坂部. Their distribution spans regions such as [[:en:Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]], [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}}, [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]], [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]], and others. The {{ill|Kusakabe Clan|ja|日下部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe Clan|qq=}} of Dazaifu, Kusakabe-no-Muraji in Bungo Province, Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Chikugo Province, Kusakabe Clan of Kawachi Province, and Kusakabe Clan of Iyo Province share a common ancestor with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inukai Clan|qq=}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2026}} ; Lineage The Kusakabe Clan is thought to originate from several genealogical lines: # {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}'s grandson, {{ill|Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko-yukushi no Mikoto|qq=}}, a member of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]]. # Descendants of {{ill|Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|ja|火闌降命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hi-no-hori no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火須勢理命}}, regarded as the progenitor of the [[:en:Hayato people|Hayato Clan]] in {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|Satsuma Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsuma Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, sharing origins with the {{ill|Inukai Clan|ja|犬養部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inukai Clan|qq=}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], who adopted the "Kusakabe" surname in {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{efn|According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Kusakabe no Ayahiko was involved in suppressing the [[:en:Prince Hoshikawa Rebellion|Prince Hoshikawa Rebellion]] under [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Yamato Sumeragi]], granting clemency to Minokone of Kawachi.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}}{{Reflist}}}} # Descendants of {{ill|Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|山代之大筒木真若王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro no Ototsutsuki Mawaka no Mikoto|qq=}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who founded the {{ill|Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Kuni-no-miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. # Descendants of {{ill|Saho-hiko no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho-hiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, a son of Hikoimasu no Mikoto, who established Kusakabe-no-Ason and Kusakabe-no-Muraji clans in Kawachi Province. # Progeny of {{ill|Prince Ōkusaka|ja|大草香皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōkusaka|qq=}}, son of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. # Descendants of [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]]'s grandson, Omoteomi no Miko (表米親王), who led the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line.{{sfn|Ota|1934|p=2024}} # Descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, son of Himusubi no Mikoto, who initiated the Kusakabe-no-Muraji lineage. # Descendants of {{ill|Watake no Mikoto|ja|若多祁命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, whose son Tasanouji founded the Kusakabe-no-Muraji of {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu Province|qq=}}. # Progeny of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], represented in Kusakabe-no-Kabane in Iyo Province. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th lineages all trace their ancestry to Hikoimasu no Mikoto. However, questions about the Kusakabe-no-Sukune line's emergence during the Be-system's formation have raised doubts about some traditions.{{sfn|Ota|1963}} A separate tradition ties the clan's name to Empress {{ill|Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|ja|草香幡梭姫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusaka Hatashiro-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, consort of Emperor Yūryaku and a daughter of Emperor Nintoku. Her name became associated with Kusakabe due to land allocations for her support, suggesting connections to military garrison-like groups. The name "Kusakabe" originates from the waka pillow word "Hinomoto no Kusaka" (日の下の草香). Similar poetic attributions include "Nagae no Hatsuse," "Kasuga no Saika," and "Flying Birds of Asuka." == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|author-link=Ota Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans, Volume 2|publisher=Surnames Publishing|year=1934|url={{NDLDC|1130938/102}}|id={{NDLBibID|47004572}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Genealogy of Japanese Clans|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|日下部氏|version=99796174|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Kusakabe clan]] [[ja:日下部氏]] [[sn:usakabe clan]] ==Merged second translation== '''Kusakabe clan (Hyūga Province)''' {{Confuse|Kusakabe clan (Hyūga Province)|Kusakabe}} {{Infobox Japanese clans (Ancient Clans) |name = Kusakabe Clan (日下部氏) |image = [[File:Japanese Crest mitumori mokkou.svg|150px]]
Mitsumori Mokko
[[Mokko mon|Mitsumori Mokko]]
(Crest of the Tajima Kusakabe Clan) |surname = |founder = |origin = |ancestor = [[Hikozakaru]]
[[Sahohiko no Mikoto]]
[[Hikoyukushi no Mikoto]]
Omotoyone no Ō? |type = [[Kōbetsu]] (Imperial lineage), [[Shinbetsu]] (Divine lineage) |main_branch = |notable_members = |descendants = [[Otagaki clan]] (Samurai)
[[Yagi clan]] (Samurai)
[[Asakura clan]] (Samurai)
[[Hashimoto clan]] (Samurai)
[[Iwakiri clan]] (Samurai)
[[Kisai clan]] (Samurai)
